| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_desktopimgdownldr_susp_execution.yml)**

> Detects a suspicious Microsoft desktopimgdownldr execution with parameters used to download files from the Internet

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Desktopimgdownldr Command
-- Sigma ID:     bb58aa4a-b80b-415a-a2c0-2f65a4c81009
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1105
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-07-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_desktopimgdownldr_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%reg delete%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\PersonalizationCSP%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment

## Devcon Execution Disabling VMware VMCI Device

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `85f520e7-6f5e-43ca-874c-222e5bf9c0de` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1543.003, T1562.001 |
| **Author** | Matt Anderson, Dray Agha, Anna Pham (Huntress) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_devcon_disable_vmci_driver.yml)**

> Detects execution of devcon.exe with commands that disable the VMware Virtual Machine Communication Interface (VMCI) device.
This can be legitimate during VMware Tools troubleshooting or driver conflicts, but may also indicate malware attempting to hijack communication with the hardware via the VMCI device.
This has been used to facilitate VMware ESXi vulnerability exploits to escape VMs and execute code on the ESXi host.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Devcon Execution Disabling VMware VMCI Device
-- Sigma ID:     85f520e7-6f5e-43ca-874c-222e5bf9c0de
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1543.003, T1562.001
-- Author:       Matt Anderson, Dray Agha, Anna Pham (Huntress)
-- Date:         2026-01-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_devcon_disable_vmci_driver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate VMware administration, Tools installation/uninstallation, or troubleshooting driver conflicts.; Automated scripts in virtualized environments for device cleanup.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% disable %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\devcon.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DevCon.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%15AD&DEV\_0740%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VMWVMCIHOSTDEV%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate VMware administration, Tools installation/uninstallation, or troubleshooting driver conflicts.; Automated scripts in virtualized environments for device cleanup.

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/devtest/devcon
- https://communities.vmware.com/t5/VMware-Workstation-Pro/VMCI-driver-issues/td-p/2866060
- https://github.com/search?q=devcon+disable+VMWVMCIHOSTDEV
- https://huntress.com/blog/esxi-vm-escape-exploit

---

## Potential DLL Sideloading Via DeviceEnroller.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e173ad47-4388-4012-ae62-bd13f71c18a8` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1574.001 |
| **Author** | @gott_cyber |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_deviceenroller_dll_sideloading.yml)**

> Detects the use of the PhoneDeepLink parameter to potentially sideload a DLL file that does not exist. This non-existent DLL file is named "ShellChromeAPI.dll".
Adversaries can drop their own renamed DLL and execute it via DeviceEnroller.exe using this parameter


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential DLL Sideloading Via DeviceEnroller.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     e173ad47-4388-4012-ae62-bd13f71c18a8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001
-- Author:       @gott_cyber
-- Date:         2022-08-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_deviceenroller_dll_sideloading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/PhoneDeepLink%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\deviceenroller.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'deviceenroller.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://mobile.twitter.com/0gtweet/status/1564131230941122561
- https://strontic.github.io/xcyclopedia/library/DeviceEnroller.exe-24BEF0D6B0ECED36BB41831759FDE18D.html

## Arbitrary MSI Download Via Devinit.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `90d50722-0483-4065-8e35-57efaadd354d` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_devinit_lolbin_usage.yml)**

> Detects a certain command line flag combination used by "devinit.exe", which can be abused as a LOLBIN to download arbitrary MSI packages on a Windows system

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary MSI Download Via Devinit.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     90d50722-0483-4065-8e35-57efaadd354d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-01-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_devinit_lolbin_usage.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -t msi-install %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i http%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/mrd0x/status/1460815932402679809
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Devinit/

## Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of ClickOnce Application

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `67bc0e75-c0a9-4cfc-8754-84a505b63c04` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dfsvc_suspicious_child_processes.yml)**

> Detects potentially suspicious child processes of a ClickOnce deployment application

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of ClickOnce Application
-- Sigma ID:     67bc0e75-c0a9-4cfc-8754-84a505b63c04
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dfsvc_suspicious_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Apps\\2.0\\%')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\calc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\notepad.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\werfault.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://posts.specterops.io/less-smartscreen-more-caffeine-ab-using-clickonce-for-trusted-code-execution-1446ea8051c5

## DirLister Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b4dc61f5-6cce-468e-a608-b48b469feaa2` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1083 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dirlister_execution.yml)**

> Detect the usage of "DirLister.exe" a utility for quickly listing folder or drive contents. It was seen used by BlackCat ransomware to create a list of accessible directories and files.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DirLister Execution
-- Sigma ID:     b4dc61f5-6cce-468e-a608-b48b469feaa2
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1083
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-08-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dirlister_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use by users
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DirLister.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\DirLister.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use by users

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/40b77d63808dd4f4eafb83949805636735a1fd15/atomics/T1083/T1083.md

## System Information Discovery via Registry Queries

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `0022869c-49f7-4ff2-ba03-85ac42ddac58` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |

| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_discovery_via_reg_queries.yml)**

> Detects attempts to query system information directly from the Windows Registry.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        System Information Discovery via Registry Queries
-- Sigma ID:     0022869c-49f7-4ff2-ba03-85ac42ddac58
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082
-- Author:       lazarg

-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_discovery_via_reg_queries.yml

-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Uninstall%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\TimeZoneInformation%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

```sql
-- ============================================================

-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_diskshadow_child_process_susp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False postitve can occur in cases where admin scripts levreage the "exec" flag to execute applications
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\diskshadow.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False postitve can occur in cases where admin scripts levreage the "exec" flag to execute applications

**References:**
- https://bohops.com/2018/03/26/diskshadow-the-return-of-vss-evasion-persistence-and-active-directory-database-extraction/
- https://www.ired.team/offensive-security/credential-access-and-credential-dumping/ntds.dit-enumeration
- https://medium.com/@cyberjyot/lolbin-execution-via-diskshadow-f6ff681a27a4
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/diskshadow
- https://www.lifars.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/GriefRansomware_Whitepaper-2.pdf
- https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/technical-analysis-crytox-ransomware
- https://research.checkpoint.com/2022/evilplayout-attack-against-irans-state-broadcaster/

## Diskshadow Script Mode - Uncommon Script Extension Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `1dde5376-a648-492e-9e54-4241dd9b0c7f` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_diskshadow_script_mode_susp_ext.yml)**

> Detects execution of "Diskshadow.exe" in script mode to execute an script with a potentially uncommon extension.
Initial baselining of the allowed extension list is required.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Diskshadow Script Mode - Uncommon Script Extension Execution
-- Sigma ID:     1dde5376-a648-492e-9e54-4241dd9b0c7f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-09-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_diskshadow_script_mode_susp_ext.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False postitve might occur with legitimate or uncommon extensions used internally. Initial baseline is required.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False postitve might occur with legitimate or uncommon extensions used internally. Initial baseline is required.

**References:**
- https://bohops.com/2018/03/26/diskshadow-the-return-of-vss-evasion-persistence-and-active-directory-database-extraction/
- https://www.ired.team/offensive-security/credential-access-and-credential-dumping/ntds.dit-enumeration
- https://medium.com/@cyberjyot/lolbin-execution-via-diskshadow-f6ff681a27a4

## Diskshadow Script Mode - Execution From Potential Suspicious Location

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `fa1a7e52-3d02-435b-81b8-00da14dd66c1` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_diskshadow_script_mode_susp_location.yml)**

> Detects execution of "Diskshadow.exe" in script mode using the "/s" flag where the script is located in a potentially suspicious location.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Diskshadow Script Mode - Execution From Potential Suspicious Location
-- Sigma ID:     fa1a7e52-3d02-435b-81b8-00da14dd66c1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-09-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_diskshadow_script_mode_susp_location.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives may occur if you execute the script from one of the paths mentioned in the rule. Apply additional filters that fits your org needs.
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-s %'
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'diskshadow.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\diskshadow.exe')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Roaming\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ProgramData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False positives may occur if you execute the script from one of the paths mentioned in the rule. Apply additional filters that fits your org needs.

**References:**
- https://bohops.com/2018/03/26/diskshadow-the-return-of-vss-evasion-persistence-and-active-directory-database-extraction/
- https://www.ired.team/offensive-security/credential-access-and-credential-dumping/ntds.dit-enumeration
- https://medium.com/@cyberjyot/lolbin-execution-via-diskshadow-f6ff681a27a4
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/diskshadow
- https://www.lifars.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/GriefRansomware_Whitepaper-2.pdf
- https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/technical-analysis-crytox-ransomware
- https://research.checkpoint.com/2022/evilplayout-attack-against-irans-state-broadcaster/

## PowerShell Web Access Feature Enabled Via DISM

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `7e8f2d3b-9c1a-4f67-b9e8-8d9006e0e51f` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1548.002 |
| **Author** | Michael Haag |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dism_enable_powershell_web_access_feature.yml)**

> Detects the use of DISM to enable the PowerShell Web Access feature, which could be used for remote access and potential abuse

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PowerShell Web Access Feature Enabled Via DISM
-- Sigma ID:     7e8f2d3b-9c1a-4f67-b9e8-8d9006e0e51f
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1548.002
-- Author:       Michael Haag
-- Date:         2024-09-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dism_enable_powershell_web_access_feature.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate PowerShell Web Access installations by administrators
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WindowsPowerShellWebAccess%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/online%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/enable-feature%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dism.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DISM.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate PowerShell Web Access installations by administrators

**References:**
- https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/dism/enable-windowsoptionalfeature
- https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-241a
- https://gist.github.com/MHaggis/7e67b659af9148fa593cf2402edebb41

---

## Dism Remove Online Package

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `43e32da2-fdd0-4156-90de-50dfd62636f9` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dism_remove.yml)**

> Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool. DISM is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dism Remove Online Package
-- Sigma ID:     43e32da2-fdd0-4156-90de-50dfd62636f9
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dism_remove.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate script
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\Dism.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/Online%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/Disable-Feature%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\DismHost.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%/Online%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%/Disable-Feature%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate script

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1562.001/T1562.001.md#atomic-test-26---disable-windows-defender-with-dism
- https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/22/h/ransomware-actor-abuses-genshin-impact-anti-cheat-driver-to-kill-antivirus.html

## DLL Sideloading by VMware Xfer Utility

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `ebea773c-a8f1-42ad-a856-00cb221966e8` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1574.001 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dll_sideload_vmware_xfer.yml)**

> Detects execution of VMware Xfer utility (VMwareXferlogs.exe) from the non-default directory which may be an attempt to sideload arbitrary DLL

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DLL Sideloading by VMware Xfer Utility
-- Sigma ID:     ebea773c-a8f1-42ad-a856-00cb221966e8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dll_sideload_vmware_xfer.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VMwareXferlogs.exe'
  AND NOT (procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\VMware\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/lockbit-ransomware-side-loads-cobalt-strike-beacon-with-legitimate-vmware-utility/

## Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e7888eb1-13b0-4616-bd99-4bc0c2b054b9` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1055 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dllhost_no_cli_execution.yml)**

> Detects a "dllhost" process spawning with no commandline arguments which is very rare to happen and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly
-- Sigma ID:     e7888eb1-13b0-4616-bd99-4bc0c2b054b9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1055
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dllhost_no_cli_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] IN ('dllhost.exe', 'dllhost')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://redcanary.com/blog/child-processes/
- https://nasbench.medium.com/what-is-the-dllhost-exe-process-actually-running-ef9fe4c19c08
- https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/static-assets/documents/malware-analysis-reports/goofy-guineapig/NCSC-MAR-Goofy-Guineapig.pdf

| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dns_susp_child_process.yml)**

> Detects an unexpected process spawning from dns.exe which may indicate activity related to remote code execution or other forms of exploitation as seen in CVE-2020-1350 (SigRed)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Unusual Child Process of dns.exe
-- Sigma ID:     a4e3d776-f12e-42c2-8510-9e6ed1f43ec3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1133
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)

-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dns.exe')
  AND NOT (procName LIKE '%\\conhost.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

## Potential Discovery Activity Via Dnscmd.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b6457d63-d2a2-4e29-859d-4e7affc153d1` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery, execution |
| **Author** | @gott_cyber |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnscmd_discovery.yml)**

> Detects an attempt to leverage dnscmd.exe to enumerate the DNS zones of a domain. DNS zones used to host the DNS records for a particular domain.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Discovery Activity Via Dnscmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     b6457d63-d2a2-4e29-859d-4e7affc153d1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery, execution
-- Author:       @gott_cyber
-- Date:         2022-07-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnscmd_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/enumrecords%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/enumzones%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/ZonePrint%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/info%'))
  AND procName LIKE '%\\dnscmd.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administration use

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/dnscmd
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/dns/dns-zones-records
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Dnscmd/

## New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed Via Dnscmd.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `f63b56ee-3f79-4b8a-97fb-5c48007e8573` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1574.001, T1112 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnscmd_install_new_server_level_plugin_dll.yml)**

> Detects the installation of a DNS plugin DLL via ServerLevelPluginDll parameter in registry, which can be used to execute code in context of the DNS server (restart required)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed Via Dnscmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     f63b56ee-3f79-4b8a-97fb-5c48007e8573
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001, T1112
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2017-05-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnscmd_install_new_server_level_plugin_dll.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dnscmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/serverlevelplugindll%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://medium.com/@esnesenon/feature-not-bug-dnsadmin-to-dc-compromise-in-one-line-a0f779b8dc83
- https://blog.3or.de/hunting-dns-server-level-plugin-dll-injection.html

## Potential Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dnx.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `81ebd28b-9607-4478-bf06-974ed9d53ed7` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218, T1027.004 |
| **Author** | Beyu Denis, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnx_execute_csharp_code.yml)**

> Detects the execution of Dnx.EXE. The Dnx utility allows for the execution of C# code.
Attackers might abuse this in order to bypass application whitelisting.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dnx.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     81ebd28b-9607-4478-bf06-974ed9d53ed7
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1027.004
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnx_execute_csharp_code.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of dnx.exe by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\dnx.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of dnx.exe by legitimate user

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Csi/
- https://enigma0x3.net/2016/11/17/bypassing-application-whitelisting-by-using-dnx-exe/

| Field | Value |
|---|---|

| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Beyu Denis, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnet_arbitrary_dll_csproj_execution.yml)**

> Detects execution of arbitrary DLLs or unsigned code via a ".csproj" files via Dotnet.EXE.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary DLL or Csproj Code Execution Via Dotnet.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     d80d5c81-04ba-45b4-84e4-92eba40e0ad3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community

-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrator usage
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administrator usage

## Binary Proxy Execution Via Dotnet-Trace.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9257c05b-4a4a-48e5-a670-b7b073cf401b` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Jimmy Bayne (@bohops) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnet_trace_lolbin_execution.yml)**

> Detects commandline arguments for executing a child process via dotnet-trace.exe

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Binary Proxy Execution Via Dotnet-Trace.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9257c05b-4a4a-48e5-a670-b7b073cf401b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Jimmy Bayne (@bohops)
-- Date:         2024-01-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnet_trace_lolbin_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of the utility in order to debug and trace a program.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-- %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%collect%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dotnet-trace.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'dotnet-trace.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate usage of the utility in order to debug and trace a program.

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/bohops/status/1740022869198037480

---

## Process Memory Dump Via Dotnet-Dump

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `53d8d3e1-ca33-4012-adf3-e05a4d652e34` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnetdump_memory_dump.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "dotnet-dump" with the "collect" flag. The execution could indicate potential process dumping of critical processes such as LSASS.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Process Memory Dump Via Dotnet-Dump
-- Sigma ID:     53d8d3e1-ca33-4012-adf3-e05a4d652e34
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnetdump_memory_dump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Process dumping is the expected behavior of the tool. So false positives are expected in legitimate usage. The PID/Process Name of the process being dumped needs to be investigated
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%collect%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dotnet-dump.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'dotnet-dump.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Process dumping is the expected behavior of the tool. So false positives are expected in legitimate usage. The PID/Process Name of the process being dumped needs to be investigated

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/diagnostics/dotnet-dump#dotnet-dump-collect
- https://twitter.com/bohops/status/1635288066909966338

## Potential Recon Activity Using DriverQuery.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9fc3072c-dc8f-4bf7-b231-18950000fadd` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_driverquery_recon.yml)**

> Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Recon Activity Using DriverQuery.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9fc3072c-dc8f-4bf7-b231-18950000fadd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_driverquery_recon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage by some scripts might trigger this as well
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%driverquery.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'drvqry.exe'))
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wscript.exe')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Temp\\%'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate usage by some scripts might trigger this as well

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2023/01/09/unwrapping-ursnifs-gifts/
- https://www.vmray.com/cyber-security-blog/analyzing-ursnif-behavior-malware-sandbox/
- https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2020/01/saigon-mysterious-ursnif-fork.html

---

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DriverQuery.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     a20def93-0709-4eae-9bd2-31206e21e6b2
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
```

## Potentially Over Permissive Permissions Granted Using Dsacls.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `01c42d3c-242d-4655-85b2-34f1739632f7` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsacls_abuse_permissions.yml)**

> Detects usage of Dsacls to grant over permissive permissions

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Over Permissive Permissions Granted Using Dsacls.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     01c42d3c-242d-4655-85b2-34f1739632f7
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsacls_abuse_permissions.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrators granting over permissive permissions to users
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /G %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dsacls.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DSACLS.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GR%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GE%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GW%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GA%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WP%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WD%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administrators granting over permissive permissions to users

**References:**
- https://ss64.com/nt/dsacls.html
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/it-pro/windows-server-2012-r2-and-2012/cc771151(v=ws.11)

## Potential Password Spraying Attempt Using Dsacls.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `bac9fb54-2da7-44e9-988f-11e9a5edbc0c` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsacls_password_spray.yml)**

> Detects possible password spraying attempts using Dsacls

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Password Spraying Attempt Using Dsacls.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     bac9fb54-2da7-44e9-988f-11e9a5edbc0c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsacls_password_spray.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of dsacls to bind to an LDAP session
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/user:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/passwd:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dsacls.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DSACLS.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of dsacls to bind to an LDAP session

**References:**
- https://www.ired.team/offensive-security-experiments/active-directory-kerberos-abuse/using-dsacls-to-check-ad-object-permissions#password-spraying-anyone
- https://ss64.com/nt/dsacls.html
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/it-pro/windows-server-2012-r2-and-2012/cc771151(v=ws.11)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Kernel Dump Using Dtrace
-- Sigma ID:     7124aebe-4cd7-4ccb-8df0-6d6b93c96795
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082

  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%syscall:::return%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lkd(%')
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\dtrace.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lkd(0)%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/0gtweet/status/1474899714290208777?s=12
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/devtest/dtrace

---

## Potential Windows Defender AV Bypass Via Dump64.EXE Rename

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `129966c9-de17-4334-a123-8b58172e664d` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003.001 |
| **Author** | Austin Songer @austinsonger, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dump64_defender_av_bypass_rename.yml)**

> Detects when a user is potentially trying to bypass the Windows Defender AV by renaming a tool to dump64.exe and placing it in the Visual Studio folder.
Currently the rule is covering only usage of procdump but other utilities can be added in order to increase coverage.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Windows Defender AV Bypass Via Dump64.EXE Rename
-- Sigma ID:     129966c9-de17-4334-a123-8b58172e664d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.001
-- Author:       Austin Songer @austinsonger, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-11-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dump64_defender_av_bypass_rename.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE ':\\Program Files%'
    AND procName LIKE '%\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\%'
    AND procName LIKE '%\\dump64.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/mrd0x/status/1460597833917251595

## DumpMinitool Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `dee0a7a3-f200-4112-a99b-952196d81e42` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036, T1003.001 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dumpminitool_execution.yml)**

> Detects the use of "DumpMinitool.exe" a tool that allows the dump of process memory via the use of the "MiniDumpWriteDump"

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DumpMinitool Execution
-- Sigma ID:     dee0a7a3-f200-4112-a99b-952196d81e42
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dumpminitool_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% Full%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% Mini%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% WithHeap%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.x86.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.arm64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('DumpMinitool.exe', 'DumpMinitool.x86.exe', 'DumpMinitool.arm64.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/mrd0x/status/1511415432888131586
- https://twitter.com/mrd0x/status/1511489821247684615
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/DumpMinitool/
- https://gist.github.com/nasbench/6d58c3c125e2fa1b8f7a09754c1b087f

## New Capture Session Launched Via DXCap.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `60f16a96-db70-42eb-8f76-16763e333590` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Beyu Denis, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dxcap_arbitrary_binary_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "DXCap.EXE" with the "-c" flag, which allows a user to launch any arbitrary binary or windows package through DXCap itself. This can be abused to potentially bypass application whitelisting.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Capture Session Launched Via DXCap.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     60f16a96-db70-42eb-8f76-16763e333590
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-10-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dxcap_arbitrary_binary_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate execution of dxcap.exe by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -c %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\DXCap.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DXCap.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate execution of dxcap.exe by legitimate user

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Dxcap/
- https://twitter.com/harr0ey/status/992008180904419328

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Esentutl Gather Credentials
-- Sigma ID:     7df1713a-1a5b-4a4b-a071-dc83b144a101
```

## Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e7be6119-fc37-43f0-ad4f-1f3f99be2f9f` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003.002, T1003.003 |
| **Author** | Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_sensitive_file_copy.yml)**

> Files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) copying

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data
-- Sigma ID:     e7be6119-fc37-43f0-ad4f-1f3f99be2f9f
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.002, T1003.003
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_sensitive_file_copy.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Copying sensitive files for legitimate use (eg. backup) or forensic investigation by legitimate incident responder or forensic investigator.
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((rawEventMsg LIKE '%vss%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /m %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /y %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\esentutl.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\esentutl.exe')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\RegBack\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\RegBack\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\RegBack\\system%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\system %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\repair\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\repair\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\repair\\system%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\windows\\ntds\\ntds.dit%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Copying sensitive files for legitimate use (eg. backup) or forensic investigation by legitimate incident responder or forensic investigator.

**References:**
- https://room362.com/post/2013/2013-06-10-volume-shadow-copy-ntdsdit-domain-hashes-remotely-part-1/
- https://www.slideshare.net/heirhabarov/hunting-for-credentials-dumping-in-windows-environment
- https://dfironthemountain.wordpress.com/2018/12/06/locked-file-access-using-esentutl-exe/
- https://github.com/LOLBAS-Project/LOLBAS/blob/2cc01b01132b5c304027a658c698ae09dd6a92bf/yml/OSBinaries/Esentutl.yml

## Esentutl Steals Browser Information

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `6a69f62d-ce75-4b57-8dce-6351eb55b362` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | collection |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1005 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_webcache.yml)**

> One way Qbot steals sensitive information is by extracting browser data from Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge by using the built-in utility esentutl.exe

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Esentutl Steals Browser Information
-- Sigma ID:     6a69f62d-ce75-4b57-8dce-6351eb55b362
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection | T1005
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_webcache.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-r%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\esentutl.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'esentutl.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\WebCache%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use

## Security Event Logging Disabled via MiniNt Registry Key - Process

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `1a4bd6af-99ac-4466-b5b2-7b72b4a05462` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.002, T1112 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_event_logging_disable_via_key_minint.yml)**

> Detects attempts to disable security event logging by adding the `MiniNt` registry key.
This key is used to disable the Windows Event Log service, which collects and stores event logs from the operating system and applications.
Adversaries may want to disable this service to prevent logging of security events that could be used to detect their activities.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Security Event Logging Disabled via MiniNt Registry Key - Process
-- Sigma ID:     1a4bd6af-99ac-4466-b5b2-7b72b4a05462
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1562.002, T1112
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-04-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_event_logging_disable_via_key_minint.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Highly Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-Item %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ni %'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Highly Unlikely

**References:**
- https://www.hackingarticles.in/defense-evasion-windows-event-logging-t1562-002/

---

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Event Viewer Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     be344333-921d-4c4d-8bb8-e584cf584780
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7

  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\eventvwr.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

## Potentially Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9f107a84-532c-41af-b005-8d12a607639f` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Bhabesh Raj, X__Junior (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_expand_cabinet_files.yml)**

> Detects the expansion or decompression of cabinet files from potentially suspicious or uncommon locations, e.g. seen in Iranian MeteorExpress related attacks

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion
-- Sigma ID:     9f107a84-532c-41af-b005-8d12a607639f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Bhabesh Raj, X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-07-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_expand_cabinet_files.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    System administrator Usage
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\expand.exe'
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '%-F:%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** System administrator Usage

**References:**
- https://labs.sentinelone.com/meteorexpress-mysterious-wiper-paralyzes-iranian-trains-with-epic-troll
- https://blog.malwarebytes.com/threat-intelligence/2021/08/new-variant-of-konni-malware-used-in-campaign-targetting-russia/

## Explorer Process Tree Break

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `949f1ffb-6e85-4f00-ae1e-c3c5b190d605` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @gott_cyber |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_break_process_tree.yml)**

> Detects a command line process that uses explorer.exe to launch arbitrary commands or binaries,
which is similar to cmd.exe /c, only it breaks the process tree and makes its parent a new instance of explorer spawning from "svchost"


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Explorer Process Tree Break
-- Sigma ID:     949f1ffb-6e85-4f00-ae1e-c3c5b190d605
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @gott_cyber
-- Date:         2019-06-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_break_process_tree.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/factory,{75dff2b7-6936-4c06-a8bb-676a7b00b24b}%')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%explorer.exe%')
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% /root,%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/CyberRaiju/status/1273597319322058752
- https://twitter.com/bohops/status/1276357235954909188?s=12
- https://twitter.com/nas_bench/status/1535322450858233858
- https://securityboulevard.com/2019/09/deobfuscating-ostap-trickbots-34000-line-javascript-downloader/

---

## File Explorer Folder Opened Using Explorer Folder Shortcut Via Shell

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c3d76afc-93df-461e-8e67-9b2bad3f2ac4` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1135 |
| **Author** | @Kostastsale |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_folder_shortcut_via_shell_binary.yml)**

> Detects the initial execution of "cmd.exe" which spawns "explorer.exe" with the appropriate command line arguments for opening the "My Computer" folder.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Explorer Folder Opened Using Explorer Folder Shortcut Via Shell
-- Sigma ID:     c3d76afc-93df-461e-8e67-9b2bad3f2ac4
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1135
-- Author:       @Kostastsale
-- Date:         2022-12-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_folder_shortcut_via_shell_binary.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
    AND procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%shell:mycomputerfolder%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://ss64.com/nt/shell.html
