## Explorer NOUACCHECK Flag

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `534f2ef7-e8a2-4433-816d-c91bccde289b` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1548.002 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_nouaccheck.yml)**

> Detects suspicious starts of explorer.exe that use the /NOUACCHECK flag that allows to run all sub processes of that newly started explorer.exe without any UAC checks

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Explorer NOUACCHECK Flag
-- Sigma ID:     534f2ef7-e8a2-4433-816d-c91bccde289b
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-02-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_nouaccheck.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Domain Controller User Logon; Unknown how many legitimate software products use that method
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/NOUACCHECK%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Domain Controller User Logon; Unknown how many legitimate software products use that method

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/ORCA6665/status/1496478087244095491

## Remote File Download Via Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `587254ee-a24b-4335-b3cd-065c0f1f4baa` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105 |
| **Author** | Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_download.yml)**

> Detects execution of "findstr" with specific flags and a remote share path. This specific set of CLI flags would allow "findstr" to download the content of the file located on the remote share as described in the LOLBAS entry.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote File Download Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     587254ee-a24b-4335-b3cd-065c0f1f4baa
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105
-- Author:       Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-10-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%findstr%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%findstr.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'FINDSTR.EXE'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Findstr/
- https://oddvar.moe/2018/04/11/putting-data-in-alternate-data-streams-and-how-to-execute-it-part-2/
- https://gist.github.com/api0cradle/cdd2d0d0ec9abb686f0e89306e277b8f

## Findstr GPP Passwords

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `91a2c315-9ee6-4052-a853-6f6a8238f90d` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1552.006 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_gpp_passwords.yml)**

> Look for the encrypted cpassword value within Group Policy Preference files on the Domain Controller. This value can be decrypted with gpp-decrypt.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Findstr GPP Passwords
-- Sigma ID:     91a2c315-9ee6-4052-a853-6f6a8238f90d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_gpp_passwords.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%cpassword%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\sysvol\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.xml%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1552.006/T1552.006.md#atomic-test-1---gpp-passwords-findstr

## Findstr Launching .lnk File

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `33339be3-148b-4e16-af56-ad16ec6c7e7b` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036, T1202, T1027.003 |
| **Author** | Trent Liffick |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_lnk.yml)**

> Detects usage of findstr to identify and execute a lnk file as seen within the HHS redirect attack

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Findstr Launching .lnk File
-- Sigma ID:     33339be3-148b-4e16-af56-ad16ec6c7e7b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1202, T1027.003
-- Author:       Trent Liffick
-- Date:         2020-05-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_lnk.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk''))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/hhsgov-open-redirect-used-by-coronavirus-phishing-to-spread-malware/

## LSASS Process Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `fe63010f-8823-4864-a96b-a7b4a0f7b929` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1552.006 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_lsass.yml)**

> Detects findstring commands that include the keyword lsass, which indicates recon actviity for the LSASS process PID

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        LSASS Process Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     fe63010f-8823-4864-a96b-a7b4a0f7b929
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_lsass.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lsass%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
  OR (rawEventMsg LIKE '% /i "lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /i lsass.exe%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr "lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr.exe "lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr.exe lsass%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://blog.talosintelligence.com/2022/08/recent-cyber-attack.html?m=1

## Permission Misconfiguration Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `47e4bab7-c626-47dc-967b-255608c9a920` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1552.006 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_recon_everyone.yml)**

> Detects usage of findstr with the "EVERYONE" or "BUILTIN" keywords.
This was seen being used in combination with "icacls" and other utilities to spot misconfigured files or folders permissions.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Permission Misconfiguration Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     47e4bab7-c626-47dc-967b-255608c9a920
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_recon_everyone.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%"Everyone"%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%'Everyone'%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%"BUILTIN\\\\"%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%'BUILTIN\\'%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%icacls %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%findstr %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Everyone%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.absolomb.com/2018-01-26-Windows-Privilege-Escalation-Guide/

## Recon Command Output Piped To Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `ccb5742c-c248-4982-8c5c-5571b9275ad3` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1057 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_recon_pipe_output.yml)**

> Detects the execution of a potential recon command where the results are piped to "findstr". This is meant to trigger on inline calls of "cmd.exe" via the "/c" or "/k" for example.
Attackers often time use this technique to extract specific information they require in their reconnaissance phase.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Recon Command Output Piped To Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     ccb5742c-c248-4982-8c5c-5571b9275ad3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1057
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2023-07-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_recon_pipe_output.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ipconfig*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%net*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%netstat*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ping*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%systeminfo*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tasklist*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%whoami*|*find%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/02cb591f75064ffe1e0df9ac3ed5972a2e491c97/atomics/T1057/T1057.md#atomic-test-6---discover-specific-process---tasklist
- https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/manage-engine-vulnerability-sector-alert-tlpclear.pdf

## Security Tools Keyword Lookup Via Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `4fe074b4-b833-4081-8f24-7dcfeca72b42` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1518.001 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_security_keyword_lookup.yml)**

> Detects execution of "findstr" to search for common names of security tools. Attackers often pipe the results of recon commands such as "tasklist" or "whoami" to "findstr" in order to filter out the results.
This detection focuses on the keywords that the attacker might use as a filter.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Security Tools Keyword Lookup Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4fe074b4-b833-4081-8f24-7dcfeca72b42
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1518.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2023-10-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_security_keyword_lookup.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% avira' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% avira"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cb' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cb"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cylance' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cylance"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% defender' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% defender"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kaspersky' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kaspersky"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kes' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kes"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% mc' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% mc"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sec' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sec"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sentinel' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sentinel"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% symantec' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% symantec"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% virus' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% virus"'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/987e3ca988ae3cff4b9f6e388c139c05bf44bbb8/atomics/T1518.001/T1518.001.md#atomic-test-1---security-software-discovery
- https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/10/18/multiple-north-korean-threat-actors-exploiting-the-teamcity-cve-2023-42793-vulnerability/
- https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/manage-engine-vulnerability-sector-alert-tlpclear.pdf

---

## Insensitive Subfolder Search Via Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `04936b66-3915-43ad-a8e5-809eadfd1141` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105 |
| **Author** | Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_subfolder_search.yml)**

> Detects execution of findstr with the "s" and "i" flags for a "subfolder" and "insensitive" search respectively. Attackers sometimes leverage this built-in utility to search the system for interesting files or filter through results of commands.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Insensitive Subfolder Search Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     04936b66-3915-43ad-a8e5-809eadfd1141
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105
-- Author:       Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-10-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_subfolder_search.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative or software activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%findstr%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%findstr.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'FINDSTR.EXE'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Administrative or software activity

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Findstr/
- https://oddvar.moe/2018/04/11/putting-data-in-alternate-data-streams-and-how-to-execute-it-part-2/
- https://gist.github.com/api0cradle/cdd2d0d0ec9abb686f0e89306e277b8f

---

## Sysmon Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude Using Findstr.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `37db85d1-b089-490a-a59a-c7b6f984f480` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1518.001 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_sysmon_discovery_via_default_altitude.yml)**

> Detects usage of "findstr" with the argument "385201". Which could indicate potential discovery of an installed Sysinternals Sysmon service using the default driver altitude (even if the name is changed).

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysmon Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude Using Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     37db85d1-b089-490a-a59a-c7b6f984f480
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1518.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_sysmon_discovery_via_default_altitude.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 385201%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1518.001/T1518.001.md#atomic-test-5---security-software-discovery---sysmon-service

## Finger.EXE Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `af491bca-e752-4b44-9c86-df5680533dbc` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1105 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_finger_execution.yml)**

> Detects execution of the "finger.exe" utility.
Finger.EXE or "TCPIP Finger Command" is an old utility that is still present on modern Windows installation. It Displays information about users on a specified remote computer (typically a UNIX computer) that is running the finger service or daemon.
Due to the old nature of this utility and the rareness of machines having the finger service. Any execution of "finger.exe" can be considered "suspicious" and worth investigating.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Finger.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     af491bca-e752-4b44-9c86-df5680533dbc
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1105
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community
-- Date:         2021-02-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_finger_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Admin activity (unclear what they do nowadays with finger.exe)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'finger.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\finger.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Admin activity (unclear what they do nowadays with finger.exe)

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/bigmacjpg/status/1349727699863011328?s=12
- https://app.any.run/tasks/40115012-a919-4208-bfed-41e82cb3dadf/
- http://hyp3rlinx.altervista.org/advisories/Windows_TCPIP_Finger_Command_C2_Channel_and_Bypassing_Security_Software.txt

---

## Filter Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `4931188c-178e-4ee7-a348-39e8a7a56821` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1070, T1562, T1562.002 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fltmc_unload_driver.yml)**

> Detect filter driver unloading activity via fltmc.exe

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Filter Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4931188c-178e-4ee7-a348-39e8a7a56821
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070, T1562, T1562.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fltmc_unload_driver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

## Sysmon Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `4d7cda18-1b12-4e52-b45c-d28653210df8` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1070, T1562, T1562.002 |
| **Author** | Kirill Kiryanov, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fltmc_unload_driver_sysmon.yml)**

> Detects possible Sysmon filter driver unloaded via fltmc.exe

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysmon Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4d7cda18-1b12-4e52-b45c-d28653210df8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070, T1562, T1562.002
-- Author:       Kirill Kiryanov, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fltmc_unload_driver_sysmon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%unload%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%sysmon%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\fltMC.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'fltMC.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://www.darkoperator.com/blog/2018/10/5/operating-offensively-against-sysmon

## Forfiles.EXE Child Process Masquerading

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `f53714ec-5077-420e-ad20-907ff9bb2958` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Anish Bogati |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_forfiles_child_process_masquerading.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "forfiles" from a non-default location, in order to potentially spawn a custom "cmd.exe" from the current working directory.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Forfiles.EXE Child Process Masquerading
-- Sigma ID:     f53714ec-5077-420e-ad20-907ff9bb2958
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Anish Bogati
-- Date:         2024-01-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_forfiles_child_process_masquerading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%.exe"'))
    AND procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '/c echo "%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.hexacorn.com/blog/2023/12/31/1-little-known-secret-of-forfiles-exe/

---

## Forfiles Command Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9aa5106d-bce3-4b13-86df-3a20f1d5cf0b` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059 |
| **Author** | Tim Rauch, Elastic, E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_forfiles_proxy_execution_.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "forfiles" with the "/c" flag.
While this is an expected behavior of the tool, it can be abused in order to proxy execution through it with any binary.
Can be used to bypass application whitelisting.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Forfiles Command Execution
-- Sigma ID:     9aa5106d-bce3-4b13-86df-3a20f1d5cf0b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Elastic, E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
-- Date:         2022-06-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_forfiles_proxy_execution_.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use via a batch script or by an administrator.
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -c %'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\forfiles.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'forfiles.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use via a batch script or by an administrator.

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Forfiles/
- https://pentestlab.blog/2020/07/06/indirect-command-execution/

---

## Uncommon FileSystem Load Attempt By Format.com

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9fb6b26e-7f9e-4517-a48b-8cac4a1b6c60` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_format_uncommon_filesystem_load.yml)**

> Detects the execution of format.com with an uncommon filesystem selection that could indicate a defense evasion activity in which "format.com" is used to load malicious DLL files or other programs.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon FileSystem Load Attempt By Format.com
-- Sigma ID:     9fb6b26e-7f9e-4517-a48b-8cac4a1b6c60
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-01-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_format_uncommon_filesystem_load.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\format.com'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/fs:%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**

## Use of FSharp Interpreters

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b96b2031-7c17-4473-afe7-a30ce714db29` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059 |
| **Author** | Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsi_fsharp_code_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of FSharp Interpreters "FsiAnyCpu.exe" and "FSi.exe"
Both can be used for AWL bypass and to execute F# code via scripts or inline.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Use of FSharp Interpreters
-- Sigma ID:     b96b2031-7c17-4473-afe7-a30ce714db29
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
-- Date:         2022-06-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsi_fsharp_code_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use by a software developer.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\fsi.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\fsianycpu.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('fsi.exe', 'fsianycpu.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use by a software developer.

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/application-security/application-control/windows-defender-application-control/design/applications-that-can-bypass-wdac
- https://bohops.com/2020/11/02/exploring-the-wdac-microsoft-recommended-block-rules-part-ii-wfc-fsi/
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/FsiAnyCpu/
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Fsi/

## Fsutil Drive Enumeration

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `63de06b9-a385-40b5-8b32-73f2b9ef84b6` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1120 |
| **Author** | Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io' |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsutil_drive_enumeration.yml)**

> Attackers may leverage fsutil to enumerated connected drives.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Fsutil Drive Enumeration
-- Sigma ID:     63de06b9-a385-40b5-8b32-73f2b9ef84b6
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1120
-- Author:       Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io'
-- Date:         2022-03-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsutil_drive_enumeration.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Certain software or administrative tasks may trigger false positives.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%drives%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\fsutil.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'fsutil.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Certain software or administrative tasks may trigger false positives.

**References:**
- Turla has used fsutil fsinfo drives to list connected drives.
- https://github.com/elastic/detection-rules/blob/414d32027632a49fb239abb8fbbb55d3fa8dd861/rules/windows/discovery_peripheral_device.toml

## Potentially Suspicious NTFS Symlink Behavior Modification

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c0b2768a-dd06-4671-8339-b16ca8d1f27f` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059, T1222.001 |
| **Author** | frack113, The DFIR Report |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsutil_symlinkevaluation.yml)**

> Detects the modification of NTFS symbolic link behavior using fsutil, which could be used to enable remote to local or remote to remote symlinks for potential attacks.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious NTFS Symlink Behavior Modification
-- Sigma ID:     c0b2768a-dd06-4671-8339-b16ca8d1f27f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059, T1222.001
-- Author:       frack113, The DFIR Report
-- Date:         2022-03-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsutil_symlinkevaluation.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage, investigate the parent process and context to determine if benign.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%fsutil%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%behavior%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%set%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SymlinkEvaluation%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('Cmd.Exe', 'PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll')))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%R2L:1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%R2R:1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%L2L:1%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate usage, investigate the parent process and context to determine if benign.

**References:**
- https://www.cybereason.com/blog/cybereason-vs.-blackcat-ransomware
- https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/fsutil-behavior
- https://thedfirreport.com/2025/06/30/hide-your-rdp-password-spray-leads-to-ransomhub-deployment/

---

## Fsutil Suspicious Invocation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `add64136-62e5-48ea-807e-88638d02df1e` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1070, T1485 |
| **Author** | Ecco, E.M. Anhaus, oscd.community |
| **Status** | stable |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsutil_usage.yml)**

> Detects suspicious parameters of fsutil (deleting USN journal, configuring it with small size, etc).
Might be used by ransomwares during the attack (seen by NotPetya and others).


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Fsutil Suspicious Invocation
-- Sigma ID:     add64136-62e5-48ea-807e-88638d02df1e
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        impact | T1070, T1485
-- Author:       Ecco, E.M. Anhaus, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-09-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fsutil_usage.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Admin activity; Scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%deletejournal%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%createjournal%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%setZeroData%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\fsutil.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'fsutil.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Admin activity; Scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/fsutil-usn
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1070/T1070.md
- https://eqllib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/analytics/c91f422a-5214-4b17-8664-c5fcf115c0a2.html
- https://github.com/albertzsigovits/malware-notes/blob/558898932c1579ff589290092a2c8febefc3a4c9/Ransomware/Lockbit.md
- https://blog.cluster25.duskrise.com/2023/05/22/back-in-black-blackbyte-nt

## Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Via FTP.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `06b401f4-107c-4ff9-947f-9ec1e7649f1e` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059, T1202 |
| **Author** | Victor Sergeev, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ftp_arbitrary_command_execution.yml)**

> Detects execution of "ftp.exe" script with the "-s" or "/s" flag and any child processes ran by "ftp.exe".

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Via FTP.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     06b401f4-107c-4ff9-947f-9ec1e7649f1e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059, T1202
-- Author:       Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ftp_arbitrary_command_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ftp.exe')
  OR (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-s:%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ftp.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ftp.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Ftp/

## Suspicious Git Clone

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `aef9d1f1-7396-4e92-a927-4567c7a495c1` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | reconnaissance |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1593.003 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_git_susp_clone.yml)**

> Detects execution of "git" in order to clone a remote repository that contain suspicious keywords which might be suspicious

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Git Clone
-- Sigma ID:     aef9d1f1-7396-4e92-a927-4567c7a495c1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        reconnaissance | T1593.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_git_susp_clone.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% clone %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%git-remote-https %'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\git.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\git-remote-https.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'git.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%exploit%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Vulns%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%vulnerability%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RemoteCodeExecution%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Invoke-%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%CVE-%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%poc-%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ProofOfConcept%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%proxyshell%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%log4shell%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%eternalblue%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%eternal-blue%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%MS17-%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://gist.githubusercontent.com/MichaelKoczwara/12faba9c061c12b5814b711166de8c2f/raw/e2068486692897b620c25fde1ea258c8218fe3d3/history.txt

## Github Self-Hosted Runner Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `5bac7a56-da88-4c27-922e-c81e113b20cb` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1102.002, T1071 |
| **Author** | Daniel Koifman (KoifSec) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_github_self_hosted_runner.yml)**

> Detects GitHub self-hosted runners executing workflows on local infrastructure that could be abused for persistence and code execution.
Shai-Hulud is an npm supply chain worm targeting CI/CD environments.
It installs runners on compromised systems to maintain access after credential theft, leveraging their access to secrets and internal networks.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Github Self-Hosted Runner Execution
-- Sigma ID:     5bac7a56-da88-4c27-922e-c81e113b20cb
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1102.002, T1071
-- Author:       Daniel Koifman (KoifSec)
-- Date:         2025-11-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_github_self_hosted_runner.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate GitHub self-hosted runner installations on designated CI/CD infrastructure; Authorized runner deployments by DevOps/Platform teams following change management; Scheduled runner updates or reconfigurations on existing build agents; Self-hosted runners that follow expected/known naming patterns; Installation via expected/known configuration management tools (reflected mostly as parent process name)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%spawnclient%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\Runner.Worker.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Runner.Worker.dll')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%configure%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\Runner.Listener.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Runner.Listener.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate GitHub self-hosted runner installations on designated CI/CD infrastructure; Authorized runner deployments by DevOps/Platform teams following change management; Scheduled runner updates or reconfigurations on existing build agents; Self-hosted runners that follow expected/known naming patterns; Installation via expected/known configuration management tools (reflected mostly as parent process name)

**References:**
- https://about.gitlab.com/blog/gitlab-discovers-widespread-npm-supply-chain-attack/
- https://securitylabs.datadoghq.com/articles/shai-hulud-2.0-npm-worm/

---

## File Decryption Using Gpg4win

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `037dcd71-33a8-4392-bb01-293c94663e5a` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpg4win_decryption.yml)**

> Detects usage of Gpg4win to decrypt files

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Decryption Using Gpg4win
-- Sigma ID:     037dcd71-33a8-4392-bb01-293c94663e5a
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-08-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpg4win_decryption.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -d %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%passphrase%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\gpg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\gpg2.exe'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://blogs.vmware.com/security/2022/11/batloader-the-evasive-downloader-malware.html
- https://www.gpg4win.de/documentation.html
- https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2022/01/19/zloader-installs-remote-access-backdoors-and-delivers-cobalt-strike/

> Detects usage of Gpg4win to encrypt files

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Encryption Using Gpg4win
-- Sigma ID:     550bbb84-ce5d-4e61-84ad-e590f0024dcd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
```

## Portable Gpg.EXE Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `77df53a5-1d78-4f32-bc5a-0e7465bd8f41` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1486 |
| **Author** | frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpg4win_portable_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "gpg.exe" from uncommon location. Often used by ransomware and loaders to decrypt/encrypt data.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Portable Gpg.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     77df53a5-1d78-4f32-bc5a-0e7465bd8f41
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1486
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-08-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpg4win_portable_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    (none)
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\gpg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\gpg2.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'gpg.exe'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**References:**
- https://www.trendmicro.com/vinfo/vn/threat-encyclopedia/malware/ransom.bat.zarlock.a
- https://securelist.com/locked-out/68960/
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/c4097dc7ed14d7f7d08c89d148c4307097e8c294/atomics/T1486/T1486.md

## File Encryption/Decryption Via Gpg4win From Suspicious Locations

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e1e0b7d7-e10b-4ee4-ac49-a4bda05d320d` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpg4win_susp_location.yml)**

> Detects usage of Gpg4win to encrypt/decrypt files located in potentially suspicious locations.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Encryption/Decryption Via Gpg4win From Suspicious Locations
-- Sigma ID:     e1e0b7d7-e10b-4ee4-ac49-a4bda05d320d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-11-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpg4win_susp_location.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-passphrase%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\gpg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\gpg2.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'GNU Privacy Guard (GnuPG)'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\PerfLogs\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Roaming\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://blogs.vmware.com/security/2022/11/batloader-the-evasive-downloader-malware.html
- https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2022/01/19/zloader-installs-remote-access-backdoors-and-delivers-cobalt-strike/

## Gpresult Display Group Policy Information

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e56d3073-83ff-4021-90fe-c658e0709e72` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1615 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpresult_execution.yml)**

> Detects cases in which a user uses the built-in Windows utility gpresult to display the Resultant Set of Policy (RSoP) information

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Gpresult Display Group Policy Information
-- Sigma ID:     e56d3073-83ff-4021-90fe-c658e0709e72
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1615
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-05-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gpresult_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\gpresult.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/z%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

## Arbitrary Binary Execution Using GUP Utility

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `d65aee4d-2292-4cea-b832-83accd6cfa43` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gup_arbitrary_binary_execution.yml)**

> Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater (gup) to launch other commands or executables

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary Binary Execution Using GUP Utility
-- Sigma ID:     d65aee4d-2292-4cea-b832-83accd6cfa43
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gup_arbitrary_binary_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Other parent binaries using GUP not currently identified
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\gup.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Other parent binaries using GUP not currently identified

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/nas_bench/status/1535322445439180803

> Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater (gup) from a process other than Notepad++ to download files.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Download Using Notepad++ GUP Utility
-- Sigma ID:     44143844-0631-49ab-97a0-96387d6b2d7c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
```

## Suspicious Child Process of Notepad++ Updater - GUP.Exe

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `bb0e87ce-c89f-4857-84fa-095e4483e9cb` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | collection |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1195.002, T1557 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gup_susp_child_process.yml)**

> Detects suspicious child process creation by the Notepad++ updater process (gup.exe).
This could indicate potential exploitation of the updater component to deliver unwanted malware.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Child Process of Notepad++ Updater - GUP.Exe
-- Sigma ID:     bb0e87ce-c89f-4857-84fa-095e4483e9cb
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        collection | T1195.002, T1557
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-02-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gup_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\gup.exe')

LIMIT 1000;
```

## Suspicious GUP Usage

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `0a4f6091-223b-41f6-8743-f322ec84930b` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1574.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gup_suspicious_execution.yml)**

> Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater in a suspicious directory, which is often used in DLL side-loading attacks

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious GUP Usage
-- Sigma ID:     0a4f6091-223b-41f6-8743-f322ec84930b
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-02-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_gup_suspicious_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Execution of tools named GUP.exe and located in folders different than Notepad++\updater
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\GUP.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Execution of tools named GUP.exe and located in folders different than Notepad++\updater

**References:**
- https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2018/09/apt10-targeting-japanese-corporations-using-updated-ttps.html

## HH.EXE Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `68c8acb4-1b60-4890-8e82-3ddf7a6dba84` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218.001 |
| **Author** | E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Dan Beavin), oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hh_chm_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "hh.exe" to open ".chm" files.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HH.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     68c8acb4-1b60-4890-8e82-3ddf7a6dba84
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.001
-- Author:       E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Dan Beavin), oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hh_chm_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives are expected with legitimate ".CHM"
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.chm%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'HH.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\hh.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False positives are expected with legitimate ".CHM"

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1218.001/T1218.001.md

> Detects the usage of "hh.exe" to execute/download remotely hosted ".chm" files.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote CHM File Download/Execution Via HH.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     f57c58b3-ee69-4ef5-9041-455bf39aaa89
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,

FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
```

| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1047, T1059.001, T1059.003, T1059.005, T1059.007, T1218, T1218.001, T1218.010, T1218.011, T1566, T1566.001 |
| **Author** | Maxim Pavlunin, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hh_html_help_susp_child_process.yml)**

> Detects a suspicious child process of a Microsoft HTML Help (HH.exe)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HTML Help HH.EXE Suspicious Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     52cad028-0ff0-4854-8f67-d25dfcbc78b4
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1047, T1059.001, T1059.003, T1059.005, T1059.007, T1218, T1218.001, T1218.010, T1218.011, T1566, T1566.001
-- Author:       Maxim Pavlunin, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-04-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hh_html_help_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\hh.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\CertReq.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\CertUtil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\installutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\MSbuild.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\MSHTA.EXE' OR procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.trustwave.com/en-us/resources/blogs/spiderlabs-blog/chm-badness-delivers-a-banking-trojan/
- https://github.com/elastic/protections-artifacts/commit/746086721fd385d9f5c6647cada1788db4aea95f#diff-27939090904026cc396b0b629c8e4314acd6f5dac40a676edbc87f4567b47eb7
- https://www.ptsecurity.com/ww-en/analytics/pt-esc-threat-intelligence/higaisa-or-winnti-apt-41-backdoors-old-and-new/
- https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/unintentional-leak-glimpse-attack-vectors-apt37
