## RDP Enable or Disable via Win32_TerminalServiceSetting WMI Class

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `4b8f6d3a-9c5e-4f2a-a7d8-6b9c3e5f2a8d` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1021.001, T1047 |
| **Author** | Daniel Koifman (KoifSec), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_rdp_enable_or_disable_via_win32_terminalservicesetting_wmi_class.yml)**

> Detects enabling or disabling of Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) using alternate methods such as WMIC or PowerShell.
In PowerShell one-liner commands, the "SetAllowTSConnections" method of the "Win32_TerminalServiceSetting" class may be used to enable or disable RDP.
In WMIC, the "rdtoggle" alias or "Win32_TerminalServiceSetting" class may be used for the same purpose.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RDP Enable or Disable via Win32_TerminalServiceSetting WMI Class
-- Sigma ID:     4b8f6d3a-9c5e-4f2a-a7d8-6b9c3e5f2a8d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        execution | T1021.001, T1047
-- Author:       Daniel Koifman (KoifSec), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-11-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_rdp_enable_or_disable_via_win32_terminalservicesetting_wmi_class.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate system administrators enabling RDP for remote support; System configuration scripts during deployment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rdtoggle%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Win32\_TerminalServiceSetting%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SetAllowTSConnections%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('wmic.exe', 'PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate system administrators enabling RDP for remote support; System configuration scripts during deployment

**References:**
- https://www.trendmicro.com/en_gb/research/22/e/uncovering-a-kingminer-botnet-attack-using-trend-micro-managed-x.html
- https://github.com/HackTricks-wiki/hacktricks/blob/72f20a3fa26775b932bd819f1824c6377802a768/src/windows-hardening/basic-cmd-for-pentesters.md#firewall
- https://github.com/Lifailon/RSA/blob/rsa/Sources/RSA-1.4.1.ps1#L1468

---

## Process Memory Dump via RdrLeakDiag.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `edadb1e5-5919-4e4c-8462-a9e643b02c4b` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003.001 |
| **Author** | Cedric MAURUGEON, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_rdrleakdiag_process_dumping.yml)**

> Detects the use of the Microsoft Windows Resource Leak Diagnostic tool "rdrleakdiag.exe" to dump process memory

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Process Memory Dump via RdrLeakDiag.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     edadb1e5-5919-4e4c-8462-a9e643b02c4b
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.001
-- Author:       Cedric MAURUGEON, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-09-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_rdrleakdiag_process_dumping.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((rawEventMsg LIKE '%/memdmp%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%fullmemdmp%')
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% /o %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /p %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\rdrleakdiag.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'RdrLeakDiag.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://www.pureid.io/dumping-abusing-windows-credentials-part-1/
- https://www.crowdstrike.com/blog/overwatch-exposes-aquatic-panda-in-possession-of-log-4-shell-exploit-tools/
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Rdrleakdiag/
- https://twitter.com/0gtweet/status/1299071304805560321?s=21
- https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/06/05/operation-crimson-palace-a-technical-deep-dive

## Windows Recovery Environment Disabled Via Reagentc

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `db1c21e4-cd66-4b4e-85ca-590f0780529c` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1490 |
| **Author** | Daniel Koifman (KoifSec), Michael Vilshin |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reagentc_disable_windows_recovery_environment.yml)**

> Detects attempts to disable windows recovery environment using Reagentc.
ReAgentc.exe is a command-line tool in Windows used to manage the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE).
It allows users to enable, disable, and configure WinRE, which is used for troubleshooting and repairing common boot issues.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Recovery Environment Disabled Via Reagentc
-- Sigma ID:     db1c21e4-cd66-4b4e-85ca-590f0780529c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       Daniel Koifman (KoifSec), Michael Vilshin
-- Date:         2025-07-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reagentc_disable_windows_recovery_environment.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrative activity
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%/disable%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reagentc.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reagentc.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administrative activity

**References:**
- https://www.elastic.co/security-labs/maas-appeal-an-infostealer-rises-from-the-ashes
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/manufacture/desktop/reagentc-command-line-options?view=windows-11

## Potential Persistence Attempt Via Run Keys Using Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `de587dce-915e-4218-aac4-835ca6af6f70` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1547.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_add_run_key.yml)**

> Detects suspicious command line reg.exe tool adding key to RUN key in Registry

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Persistence Attempt Via Run Keys Using Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     de587dce-915e-4218-aac4-835ca6af6f70
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1547.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-06-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_add_run_key.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Discord
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%reg%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Discord

**References:**
- https://app.any.run/tasks/9c0f37bc-867a-4314-b685-e101566766d7/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/setupapi/run-and-runonce-registry-keys
- https://github.com/HackTricks-wiki/hacktricks/blob/e4c7b21b8f36c97c35b7c622732b38a189ce18f7/src/windows-hardening/windows-local-privilege-escalation/privilege-escalation-with-autorun-binaries.md

## Add SafeBoot Keys Via Reg Utility

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `d7662ff6-9e97-4596-a61d-9839e32dee8d` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_add_safeboot.yml)**

> Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "add" or "copy" flags on safe boot registry keys. Often used by attacker to allow the ransomware to work in safe mode as some security products do not

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Add SafeBoot Keys Via Reg Utility
-- Sigma ID:     d7662ff6-9e97-4596-a61d-9839e32dee8d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_add_safeboot.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% copy %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://redacted.com/blog/bianlian-ransomware-gang-gives-it-a-go/

---

## Suspicious Reg Add BitLocker

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `0e0255bf-2548-47b8-9582-c0955c9283f5` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1486 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_bitlocker.yml)**

> Detects suspicious addition to BitLocker related registry keys via the reg.exe utility

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Reg Add BitLocker
-- Sigma ID:     0e0255bf-2548-47b8-9582-c0955c9283f5
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1486
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_bitlocker.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%REG%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ADD%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\FVE%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/f%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%EnableBDEWithNoTPM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UseAdvancedStartup%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UseTPM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UseTPMKey%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UseTPMKeyPIN%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RecoveryKeyMessageSource%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UseTPMPIN%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RecoveryKeyMessage%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2021/11/15/exchange-exploit-leads-to-domain-wide-ransomware/

## Dropping Of Password Filter DLL

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b7966f4a-b333-455b-8370-8ca53c229762` |

| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1556.002 |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_credential_access_via_password_filter.yml)**

> Detects dropping of dll files in system32 that may be used to retrieve user credentials from LSASS

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dropping Of Password Filter DLL
-- Sigma ID:     b7966f4a-b333-455b-8370-8ca53c229762
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1556.002
-- Author:       Sreeman
-- Date:         2020-10-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_credential_access_via_password_filter.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%scecli\\0*%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%reg add%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

## Suspicious Windows Defender Folder Exclusion Added Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `48917adc-a28e-4f5d-b729-11e75da8941f` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_defender_exclusion.yml)**

> Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to add Defender folder exclusions. Qbot has been seen using this technique to add exclusions for folders within AppData and ProgramData.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Windows Defender Folder Exclusion Added Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     48917adc-a28e-4f5d-b729-11e75da8941f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_defender_exclusion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Exclusions\\Paths%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Microsoft Antimalware\\Exclusions\\Paths%'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ADD %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/t %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%REG\_DWORD %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/d %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%0%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/02/07/qbot-likes-to-move-it-move-it/
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/threats/qbot/

## RunMRU Registry Key Deletion

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c11aecef-9c37-45a6-9c07-bc0782f963fd` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1070.003 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_delete_runmru.yml)**

> Detects deletion of the RunMRU registry key, which stores the history of commands executed via the Run dialog.
In the clickfix techniques, the phishing lures instruct users to open a run dialog through (Win + R) and execute malicious commands.
Adversaries may delete this key to cover their tracks after executing commands.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RunMRU Registry Key Deletion
-- Sigma ID:     c11aecef-9c37-45a6-9c07-bc0782f963fd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1070.003
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-09-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_delete_runmru.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% del%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\RunMRU%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/coldriver-updates-arsenal-baitswitch-and-simplefix

---

## Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Using Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `8cbc9475-8d05-4e27-9c32-df960716c701` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence, impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1112, T1491.001 |
| **Author** | Stephen Lincoln @slincoln-aiq (AttackIQ) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_desktop_background_change.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "reg.exe" to alter registry keys that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Using Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     8cbc9475-8d05-4e27-9c32-df960716c701
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, impact | T1112, T1491.001
-- Author:       Stephen Lincoln @slincoln-aiq (AttackIQ)
-- Date:         2023-12-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_desktop_background_change.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative scripts that change the desktop background to a company logo or other image.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Control Panel\\Desktop%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%CurrentVersion\\Policies\\ActiveDesktop%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Administrative scripts that change the desktop background to a company logo or other image.

**References:**
- https://www.attackiq.com/2023/09/20/emulating-rhysida/
- https://research.checkpoint.com/2023/the-rhysida-ransomware-activity-analysis-and-ties-to-vice-society/
- https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/23/h/an-overview-of-the-new-rhysida-ransomware.html
- https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/a864282fea5a536510ae86c77ce46f7827687783628e4f2ceb5bf2c41b8cd3c6/behavior
- https://admx.help/?Category=Windows_10_2016&Policy=Microsoft.Policies.WindowsDesktop::Wallpaper
- https://admx.help/?Category=Windows_10_2016&Policy=Microsoft.Policies.ControlPanelDisplay::CPL_Personalization_NoDesktopBackgroundUI

```sql
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_direct_asep_registry_keys_modification.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Discord
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\Userinit%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\Shell%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\system\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot\\AlternateShell%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Discord

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1547.001/T1547.001.md
- https://github.com/HackTricks-wiki/hacktricks/blob/e4c7b21b8f36c97c35b7c622732b38a189ce18f7/src/windows-hardening/windows-local-privilege-escalation/privilege-escalation-with-autorun-binaries.md

## Disabling Windows Defender WMI Autologger Session via Reg.exe

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-a7b8-c9d0-e1f2a3b4c5d6` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | Matt Anderson (Huntress) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_disable_defender_wmi_autologger.yml)**

> Detects the use of reg.exe to disable the Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) Autologger session for Windows Defender API and Audit events.
By setting the 'Start' value to '0' for the 'DefenderApiLogger' or 'DefenderAuditLogger' session, an attacker can prevent these critical security events
from being logged, effectively blinding monitoring tools that rely on this data. This is a powerful defense evasion technique.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Disabling Windows Defender WMI Autologger Session via Reg.exe
-- Sigma ID:     a1b2c3d4-e5f6-a7b8-c9d0-e1f2a3b4c5d6
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Matt Anderson (Huntress)
-- Date:         2025-07-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_disable_defender_wmi_autologger.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Highly unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Highly unlikely

**References:**
- https://research.splunk.com/endpoint/76406a0f-f5e0-4167-8e1f-337fdc0f1b0c/
- https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/etw/configuring-and-starting-an-autologger-session
- https://thedfirreport.com/2021/10/18/icedid-to-xinglocker-ransomware-in-24-hours/
- https://blog.malwarebytes.com/malwarebytes-news/2021/02/lazyscripter-from-empire-to-double-rat/
- https://www.binarly.io/blog/design-issues-of-modern-edrs-bypassing-etw-based-solutions

---

## Security Service Disabled Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `5e95028c-5229-4214-afae-d653d573d0ec` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), John Lambert (idea), elhoim |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_disable_sec_services.yml)**

> Detects execution of "reg.exe" to disable security services such as Windows Defender.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Security Service Disabled Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     5e95028c-5229-4214-afae-d653d573d0ec
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), John Lambert (idea), elhoim
-- Date:         2021-07-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_disable_sec_services.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%d 4%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%v Start%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppIDSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\MsMpSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\NisSrv%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SecurityHealthService%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Sense%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\UsoSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdBoot%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdFilter%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdNisDrv%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdNisSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WinDefend%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\wscsvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\wuauserv%')))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%reg%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/JohnLaTwC/status/1415295021041979392
- https://github.com/gordonbay/Windows-On-Reins/blob/e587ac7a0407847865926d575e3c46f68cf7c68d/wor.ps1
- https://vms.drweb.fr/virus/?i=24144899
- https://bidouillesecurity.com/disable-windows-defender-in-powershell/

## Dumping of Sensitive Hives Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `fd877b94-9bb5-4191-bb25-d79cbd93c167` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003.002, T1003.004, T1003.005 |
| **Author** | Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Endgame, JHasenbusch, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_dumping_sensitive_hives.yml)**

> Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to dump sensitive registry hives. This includes SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY hives.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dumping of Sensitive Hives Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     fd877b94-9bb5-4191-bb25-d79cbd93c167
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.002, T1003.004, T1003.005
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Endgame, JHasenbusch, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, frack113
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_dumping_sensitive_hives.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% save %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% export %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ˢave %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% eˣport %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\system%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢystem%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\syˢtem%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢyˢtem%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢecurity%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hklm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hk˪m%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_local\_machine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_˪ocal\_machine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_loca˪\_machine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_˪oca˪\_machine%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation

**References:**
- https://www.slideshare.net/heirhabarov/hunting-for-credentials-dumping-in-windows-environment
- https://eqllib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/analytics/aed95fc6-5e3f-49dc-8b35-06508613f979.html
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1003/T1003.md
- https://www.wietzebeukema.nl/blog/windows-command-line-obfuscation
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1003.002/T1003.002.md#atomic-test-1---registry-dump-of-sam-creds-and-secrets

## Windows Recall Feature Enabled Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `817f252c-5143-4dae-b418-48c3e9f63728` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | collection |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1113 |
| **Author** | Sajid Nawaz Khan |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_enable_windows_recall.yml)**

> Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature via registry manipulation.
Windows Recall can be enabled by deleting the existing "DisableAIDataAnalysis" value, or setting it to 0.
Adversaries may enable Windows Recall as part of post-exploitation discovery and collection activities.
This rule assumes that Recall is already explicitly disabled on the host, and subsequently enabled by the adversary.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Recall Feature Enabled Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     817f252c-5143-4dae-b418-48c3e9f63728
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection | T1113
-- Author:       Sajid Nawaz Khan
-- Date:         2024-06-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_enable_windows_recall.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use/activation of Windows Recall
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Microsoft\\Windows\\WindowsAI%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DisableAIDataAnalysis%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use/activation of Windows Recall

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/manage-recall
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/policy-csp-windowsai#disableaidataanalysis

---

## Enumeration for Credentials in Registry

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e0b0c2ab-3d52-46d9-8cb7-049dc775fbd1` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1552.002 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_enumeration_for_credentials_in_registry.yml)**

> Adversaries may search the Registry on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.
The Windows Registry stores configuration information that can be used by the system or other programs.
Adversaries may query the Registry looking for credentials and passwords that have been stored for use by other programs or services


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Enumeration for Credentials in Registry
-- Sigma ID:     e0b0c2ab-3d52-46d9-8cb7-049dc775fbd1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_enumeration_for_credentials_in_registry.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% query %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/t %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%REG\_SZ%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/s%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/f %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKLM%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/f %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKCU%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKCU\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1552.002/T1552.002.md

## Potential Suspicious Registry File Imported Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `62e0298b-e994-4189-bc87-bc699aa62d97` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1112 |
| **Author** | frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_import_from_suspicious_paths.yml)**

> Detects the import of '.reg' files from suspicious paths using the 'reg.exe' utility

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Suspicious Registry File Imported Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     62e0298b-e994-4189-bc87-bc699aa62d97
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
-- Date:         2022-08-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_import_from_suspicious_paths.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate import of keys
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% import %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Users\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%temp\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%tmp\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%appdata\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\ProgramData\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate import of keys

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/reg-import

## RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `28ac00d6-22d9-4a3c-927f-bbd770104573` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1112 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_lsa_disable_restricted_admin.yml)**

> Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation
-- Sigma ID:     28ac00d6-22d9-4a3c-927f-bbd770104573
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2023-01-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_lsa_disable_restricted_admin.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DisableRestrictedAdmin%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/a8e3cf63e97b973a25903d3df9fd55da6252e564/atomics/T1112/T1112.md
- https://social.technet.microsoft.com/wiki/contents/articles/32905.remote-desktop-services-enable-restricted-admin-mode.aspx
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/05/09/seo-poisoning-a-gootloader-story/

## LSA PPL Protection Disabled Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `8c0eca51-0f88-4db2-9183-fdfb10c703f9` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.010 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_lsa_ppl_protection_disabled.yml)**

> Detects the usage of the "reg.exe" utility to disable PPL protection on the LSA process

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        LSA PPL Protection Disabled Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     8c0eca51-0f88-4db2-9183-fdfb10c703f9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-03-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_lsa_ppl_protection_disabled.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /d 0%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /v RunAsPPL %'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/03/21/apt35-automates-initial-access-using-proxyshell/

---

## Suspicious Query of MachineGUID

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `f5240972-3938-4e56-8e4b-e33893176c1f` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1082 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_machineguid.yml)**

> Use of reg to get MachineGuid information

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Query of MachineGUID
-- Sigma ID:     f5240972-3938-4e56-8e4b-e33893176c1f
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_machineguid.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Cryptography%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%MachineGuid%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**

## Modify Group Policy Settings

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `ada4b0c4-758b-46ac-9033-9004613a150d` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1484.001 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_modify_group_policy_settings.yml)**

> Detect malicious GPO modifications can be used to implement many other malicious behaviors.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Modify Group Policy Settings
-- Sigma ID:     ada4b0c4-758b-46ac-9033-9004613a150d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1484.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_modify_group_policy_settings.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTimeDC%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTimeOffsetDC%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTime%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTimeOffset%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%EnableSmartScreen%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ShellSmartScreenLevel%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\System%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/40b77d63808dd4f4eafb83949805636735a1fd15/atomics/T1484.001/T1484.001.md

## Enable LM Hash Storage - ProcCreation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `98dedfdd-8333-49d4-9f23-d7018cccae53` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1112 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_nolmhash.yml)**

> Detects changes to the "NoLMHash" registry value in order to allow Windows to store LM Hashes.
By setting this registry value to "0" (DWORD), Windows will be allowed to store a LAN manager hash of your password in Active Directory and local SAM databases.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Enable LM Hash Storage - ProcCreation
-- Sigma ID:     98dedfdd-8333-49d4-9f23-d7018cccae53
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-12-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_nolmhash.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%NoLMHash%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 0%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-347a
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/windows-security/prevent-windows-store-lm-hash-password
- https://www.sans.org/blog/protecting-privileged-domain-accounts-lm-hashes-the-good-the-bad-and-the-ugly/

## Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `970007b7-ce32-49d0-a4a4-fbef016950bd` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1012, T1007 |
| **Author** | Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_query_registry.yml)**

> Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to query reconnaissance information from the registry. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about credentials, the system, configuration, and installed software.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     970007b7-ce32-49d0-a4a4-fbef016950bd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1012, T1007
-- Author:       Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_query_registry.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Discord
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%query%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\windows%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%winlogon\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\shellServiceObjectDelayLoad%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\policies\\explorer\\run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentcontrolset\\services%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Discord

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1012/T1012.md

---

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Tampering With RDP Related Registry Keys Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     0d5675be-bc88-4172-86d3-1e96a4476536
```

## Suspicious ScreenSave Change by Reg.exe

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `0fc35fc3-efe6-4898-8a37-0b233339524f` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1546.002 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_screensaver.yml)**

> Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity.
Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious ScreenSave Change by Reg.exe
-- Sigma ID:     0fc35fc3-efe6-4898-8a37-0b233339524f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_screensaver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    GPO
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKEY\_CURRENT\_USER\\Control Panel\\Desktop%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKCU\\Control Panel\\Desktop%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** GPO

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1546.002/T1546.002.md
- https://www.welivesecurity.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/eset-gazer.pdf

## Changing Existing Service ImagePath Value Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9b0b7ac3-6223-47aa-a3fd-e8f211e637db` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1574.011 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_service_imagepath_change.yml)**

> Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.
Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.
Windows stores local service configuration information in the Registry under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Changing Existing Service ImagePath Value Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9b0b7ac3-6223-47aa-a3fd-e8f211e637db
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.011
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_service_imagepath_change.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ImagePath %'))
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -d %')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1574.011/T1574.011.md#atomic-test-2---service-imagepath-change-with-regexe

## Reg Add Suspicious Paths

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b7e2a8d4-74bb-4b78-adc9-3f92af2d4829` |

> Detects when an adversary uses the reg.exe utility to add or modify new keys or subkeys

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Reg Add Suspicious Paths
-- Sigma ID:     b7e2a8d4-74bb-4b78-adc9-3f92af2d4829
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112, T1562.001

-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_susp_paths.yml

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppDataLow\\Software\\Microsoft\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\OOBE%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\Currentversion\\Winlogon%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SecurityProviders\\WDigest%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Rare legitimate add to registry via cli (to these locations)

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/40b77d63808dd4f4eafb83949805636735a1fd15/atomics/T1112/T1112.md
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/40b77d63808dd4f4eafb83949805636735a1fd15/atomics/T1562.001/T1562.001.md
- https://www.absolomb.com/2018-01-26-Windows-Privilege-Escalation-Guide/

## System Language Discovery via Reg.Exe

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c43a5405-e8e1-4221-9ac9-dbe3fa14e886` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1614.001 |
| **Author** | Marco Pedrinazzi (@pedrinazziM) (InTheCyber) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_system_language_discovery.yml)**

> Detects the usage of Reg.Exe to query system language settings.
Attackers may discover the system language to determine the geographic location of victims, customize payloads for specific regions,
or avoid targeting certain locales to evade detection.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        System Language Discovery via Reg.Exe
-- Sigma ID:     c43a5405-e8e1-4221-9ac9-dbe3fa14e886
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1614.001
-- Author:       Marco Pedrinazzi (@pedrinazziM) (InTheCyber)
-- Date:         2026-01-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_system_language_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%query%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Control\\Nls\\Language%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://scythe.io/threat-thursday/threatthursday-darkside-ransomware

## Suspicious Windows Defender Registry Key Tampering Via Reg.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `452bce90-6fb0-43cc-97a5-affc283139b3` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_windows_defender_tamper.yml)**

> Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to tamper with different Windows Defender registry keys in order to disable some important features related to protection and detection


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Windows Defender Registry Key Tampering Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     452bce90-6fb0-43cc-97a5-affc283139b3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-03-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_windows_defender_tamper.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Rare legitimate use by administrators to test software (should always be investigated)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Rare legitimate use by administrators to test software (should always be investigated)

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/03/21/apt35-automates-initial-access-using-proxyshell/

## Write Protect For Storage Disabled

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `75f7a0e2-7154-4c4d-9eae-5cdb4e0a5c13` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562 |
| **Author** | Sreeman |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_write_protect_for_storage_disabled.yml)**

> Detects applications trying to modify the registry in order to disable any write-protect property for storage devices.
This could be a precursor to a ransomware attack and has been an observed technique used by cypherpunk group.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Write Protect For Storage Disabled
-- Sigma ID:     75f7a0e2-7154-4c4d-9eae-5cdb4e0a5c13
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562
-- Author:       Sreeman
-- Date:         2021-06-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_write_protect_for_storage_disabled.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Write Protection%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%0%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%storage%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.manageengine.com/products/desktop-central/os-imaging-deployment/media-is-write-protected.html

## RegAsm.EXE Execution Without CommandLine Flags or Files

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `651f87f7-12db-47f9-84c5-f27b081b94b6` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218.009 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regasm_no_flag_or_dll_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of "RegAsm.exe" without a commandline flag or file, which might indicate potential process injection activity.
Usually "RegAsm.exe" should point to a dedicated DLL file or call the help with the "/?" flag.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RegAsm.EXE Execution Without CommandLine Flags or Files
-- Sigma ID:     651f87f7-12db-47f9-84c5-f27b081b94b6
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1218.009
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2025-06-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regasm_no_flag_or_dll_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Regasm by developers.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm.exe"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm.exe''))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\RegAsm.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'RegAsm.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of Regasm by developers.

**References:**
- https://www.mcafee.com/blogs/other-blogs/mcafee-labs/agent-teslas-unique-approach-vbs-and-steganography-for-delivery-and-intrusion/
- https://www.zscaler.fr/blogs/security-research/threat-actors-exploit-cve-2017-11882-deliver-agent-tesla
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/tools/regasm-exe-assembly-registration-tool
- https://app.any.run/tasks/ea944b89-69d8-49c8-ac1f-5c76ad300db2
