```sql
metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SQLsafe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SQLSERVERAGENT%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SQLTELEMETRY%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SQLWriter%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SSISTELEMETRY130%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SstpSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%storflt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%svcGenericHost%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%swc\_service%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%swi\_filter%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%swi\_service%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%swi\_update%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Symantec%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%sysmon%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TeamViewer%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Telemetryserver%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ThreatLockerService%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TMBMServer%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TmCCSF%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TmFilter%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TMiCRCScanService%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tmlisten%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TMLWCSService%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TmPfw%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TmPreFilter%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TmProxy%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TMSmartRelayService%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tmusa%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Tomcat%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Trend Micro Deep Security Manager%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TrueKey%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UFNet%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UI0Detect%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UniFi%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UTODetect%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%vds%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Veeam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VeeamDeploySvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Veritas System Recovery%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%vmic%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VMTools%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%vmvss%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VSApiNt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VSS%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%W3Svc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wbengine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WdNisSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WeanClOudSve%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Weems JY%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WinDefend%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wmms%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wozyprobackup%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WPFFontCache\_v0400%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WRSVC%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wsbexchange%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WSearch%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wscsvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Zoolz 2 Service%'))
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% delete %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.delete()%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% pause %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% stop %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Stop-Service %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Remove-Service %')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%start=disabled%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe', 'PowerShell_ISE.EXE', 'PowerShell.EXE', 'psservice.exe', 'pwsh.dll', 'sc.exe', 'wmic.exe')))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\PowerShell\_ISE.EXE' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\PsService.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\PsService64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Administrators or tools shutting down the services due to upgrade or removal purposes. If you experience some false positive, please consider adding filters to the parent process launching this command and not removing the entry

**References:**
- https://www.trendmicro.com/content/dam/trendmicro/global/en/research/22/h/ransomware-actor-abuses-genshin-impact-anti-cheat-driver-to-kill-antivirus/Genshin%20Impact%20Figure%2010.jpg
- https://www.trellix.com/en-sg/about/newsroom/stories/threat-labs/lockergoga-ransomware-family-used-in-targeted-attacks.html
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1562.001/T1562.001.md
- https://research.nccgroup.com/2022/08/19/back-in-black-unlocking-a-lockbit-3-0-ransomware-attack/
- https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/38283b775552da8981452941ea74191aa0d203edd3f61fb2dee7b0aea3514955
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/cimwin32prov/delete-method-in-class-win32-service

## Shadow Copies Creation Using Operating Systems Utilities

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b17ea6f7-6e90-447e-a799-e6c0a493d6ce` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003, T1003.002, T1003.003 |
| **Author** | Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_shadow_copies_creation.yml)**

> Shadow Copies creation using operating systems utilities, possible credential access

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Shadow Copies Creation Using Operating Systems Utilities
-- Sigma ID:     b17ea6f7-6e90-447e-a799-e6c0a493d6ce
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003, T1003.002, T1003.003
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_shadow_copies_creation.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrator working with shadow copies, access for backup purposes
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%shadow%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%create%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\vssadmin.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll', 'wmic.exe', 'VSSADMIN.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administrator working with shadow copies, access for backup purposes

**References:**
- https://www.slideshare.net/heirhabarov/hunting-for-credentials-dumping-in-windows-environment
- https://www.trustwave.com/en-us/resources/blogs/spiderlabs-blog/tutorial-for-ntds-goodness-vssadmin-wmis-ntdsdit-system/

---

## Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c947b146-0abc-4c87-9c64-b17e9d7274a2` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1070, T1490 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades) |
| **Status** | stable |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_shadow_copies_deletion.yml)**

> Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
-- Sigma ID:     c947b146-0abc-4c87-9c64-b17e9d7274a2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        impact | T1070, T1490
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_shadow_copies_deletion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate Administrator deletes Shadow Copies using operating systems utilities for legitimate reason; LANDesk LDClient Ivanti-PSModule (PS EncodedCommand)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate Administrator deletes Shadow Copies using operating systems utilities for legitimate reason; LANDesk LDClient Ivanti-PSModule (PS EncodedCommand)

**References:**

## Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `3a6586ad-127a-4d3b-a677-1e6eacdf8fde` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059.005, T1059.001, T1218 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_shell_spawn_susp_program.yml)**

> Detects suspicious child processes of a Windows shell and scripting processes such as wscript, rundll32, powershell, mshta...etc.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs
-- Sigma ID:     3a6586ad-127a-4d3b-a677-1e6eacdf8fde
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.005, T1059.001, T1218
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton
-- Date:         2018-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_shell_spawn_susp_program.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative scripts; Microsoft SCCM
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wmiprvse.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe'))
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nslookup.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Administrative scripts; Microsoft SCCM

**References:**
- https://mgreen27.github.io/posts/2018/04/02/DownloadCradle.html

## Process Creation Using Sysnative Folder

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `3c1b5fb0-c72f-45ba-abd1-4d4c353144ab` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1055 |
| **Author** | Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_sysnative.yml)**

> Detects process creation events that use the Sysnative folder (common for CobaltStrike spawns)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Process Creation Using Sysnative Folder
-- Sigma ID:     3c1b5fb0-c72f-45ba-abd1-4d4c353144ab
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1055
-- Author:       Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_sysnative.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Sysnative\\%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Sysnative\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2021/08/29/cobalt-strike-a-defenders-guide/

## System File Execution Location Anomaly

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `e4a6b256-3e47-40fc-89d2-7a477edd6915` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_system_exe_anomaly.yml)**

> Detects the execution of a Windows system binary that is usually located in the system folder from an uncommon location.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        System File Execution Location Anomaly
-- Sigma ID:     e4a6b256-3e47-40fc-89d2-7a477edd6915
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2017-11-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_system_exe_anomaly.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\atbroker.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\audiodg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bcdedit.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certreq.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmstp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\conhost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\consent.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\csrss.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dashost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\defrag.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dfrgui.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dism.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dllhst3g.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dwm.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\eventvwr.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\fsquirt.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\finger.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\logonui.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\LsaIso.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\lsass.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\lsm.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ntoskrnl.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\runonce.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\RuntimeBroker.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\services.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sihost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\smartscreen.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\smss.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\spoolsv.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\svchost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\taskhost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\taskhostw.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\Taskmgr.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\userinit.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\werfault.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\werfaultsecure.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wininit.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winlogon.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winver.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wlanext.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wsl.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wsmprovhost.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/GelosSnake/status/934900723426439170
- https://asec.ahnlab.com/en/39828/
- https://www.splunk.com/en_us/blog/security/inno-setup-malware-redline-stealer-campaign.html

> Detects a suspicious process creation as SYSTEM user (suspicious program or command line parameter)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious SYSTEM User Process Creation
-- Sigma ID:     2617e7ed-adb7-40ba-b0f3-8f9945fe6c09
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test

SELECT

  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

## Suspicious SYSVOL Domain Group Policy Access

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `05f3c945-dcc8-4393-9f3d-af65077a8f86` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1552.006 |
| **Author** | Markus Neis, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_sysvol_access.yml)**

> Detects Access to Domain Group Policies stored in SYSVOL

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious SYSVOL Domain Group Policy Access
-- Sigma ID:     05f3c945-dcc8-4393-9f3d-af65077a8f86
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       Markus Neis, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
-- Date:         2018-04-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_sysvol_access.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSVOL\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\policies\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Administrative activity

**References:**
- https://adsecurity.org/?p=2288
- https://www.hybrid-analysis.com/sample/f2943f5e45befa52fb12748ca7171d30096e1d4fc3c365561497c618341299d5?environmentId=100

---

## Tasks Folder Evasion

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `cc4e02ba-9c06-48e2-b09e-2500cace9ae0` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence, execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1574.001 |
| **Author** | Sreeman |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_task_folder_evasion.yml)**

> The Tasks folder in system32 and syswow64 are globally writable paths.
Adversaries can take advantage of this and load or influence any script hosts or ANY .NET Application
in Tasks to load and execute a custom assembly into cscript, wscript, regsvr32, mshta, eventvwr


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Tasks Folder Evasion
-- Sigma ID:     cc4e02ba-9c06-48e2-b09e-2500cace9ae0
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, execution | T1574.001
-- Author:       Sreeman
-- Date:         2020-01-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_task_folder_evasion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%echo %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%type %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%file createnew%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% C:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% C:\\Windows\\SysWow64\\Tasks\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/subTee/status/1216465628946563073
- https://gist.github.com/am0nsec/8378da08f848424e4ab0cc5b317fdd26

---

## Malicious Windows Script Components File Execution by TAEF Detection

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `634b00d5-ccc3-4a06-ae3b-0ec8444dd51b` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_use_of_te_bin.yml)**

> Windows Test Authoring and Execution Framework (TAEF) framework allows you to run automation by executing tests files written on different languages (C, C#, Microsoft COM Scripting interfaces
Adversaries may execute malicious code (such as WSC file with VBScript, dll and so on) directly by running te.exe


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Malicious Windows Script Components File Execution by TAEF Detection
-- Sigma ID:     634b00d5-ccc3-4a06-ae3b-0ec8444dd51b
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_use_of_te_bin.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    It's not an uncommon to use te.exe directly to execute legal TAEF tests
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\te.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\te.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\te.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** It's not an uncommon to use te.exe directly to execute legal TAEF tests

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Te/
- https://twitter.com/pabraeken/status/993298228840992768
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/taef/

---

## Malicious PE Execution by Microsoft Visual Studio Debugger

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `15c7904e-6ad1-4a45-9b46-5fb25df37fd2` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Agro (@agro_sev), Ensar Şamil (@sblmsrsn), oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_use_of_vsjitdebugger_bin.yml)**

> There is an option for a MS VS Just-In-Time Debugger "vsjitdebugger.exe" to launch specified executable and attach a debugger.
This option may be used adversaries to execute malicious code by signed verified binary.
The debugger is installed alongside with Microsoft Visual Studio package.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Malicious PE Execution by Microsoft Visual Studio Debugger
-- Sigma ID:     15c7904e-6ad1-4a45-9b46-5fb25df37fd2
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Agro (@agro_sev), Ensar Şamil (@sblmsrsn), oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_use_of_vsjitdebugger_bin.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    The process spawned by vsjitdebugger.exe is uncommon.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\vsjitdebugger.exe')
  AND NOT (procName LIKE '%\\vsimmersiveactivatehelper*.exe'))
  OR procName LIKE '%\\devenv.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** The process spawned by vsjitdebugger.exe is uncommon.

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/pabraeken/status/990758590020452353
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Vsjitdebugger/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/debugger/debug-using-the-just-in-time-debugger?view=vs-2019

---

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Userinit Child Process

-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
```

## Suspicious Velociraptor Child Process

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `4bc90587-e6ca-4b41-be0b-ed4d04e4ed0c` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1219 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_velociraptor_child_process.yml)**

> Detects the suspicious use of the Velociraptor DFIR tool to execute other tools or download additional payloads, as seen in a campaign where it was abused for remote access and to stage further attacks.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Velociraptor Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     4bc90587-e6ca-4b41-be0b-ed4d04e4ed0c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1219
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-08-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_velociraptor_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrators or incident responders might use Velociraptor to execute scripts or tools. However, the combination of Velociraptor spawning these specific processes with these command lines is suspicious. Tuning may be required to exclude known administrative actions or specific scripts.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Velociraptor.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administrators or incident responders might use Velociraptor to execute scripts or tools. However, the combination of Velociraptor spawning these specific processes with these command lines is suspicious. Tuning may be required to exclude known administrative actions or specific scripts.

**References:**
- https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2025/08/26/velociraptor-incident-response-tool-abused-for-remote-access/

---

## Weak or Abused Passwords In CLI

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `91edcfb1-2529-4ac2-9ecc-7617f895c7e4` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_weak_or_abused_passwords.yml)**

> Detects weak passwords or often abused passwords (seen used by threat actors) via the CLI.
An example would be a threat actor creating a new user via the net command and providing the password inline


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Weak or Abused Passwords In CLI
-- Sigma ID:     91edcfb1-2529-4ac2-9ecc-7617f895c7e4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_weak_or_abused_passwords.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of the passwords by users via commandline (should be discouraged); Other currently unknown false positives
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%123456789%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%123123qwE%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Asd123.aaaa%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Decryptme%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%P@ssw0rd!%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Pass8080%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%password123%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%test@202%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate usage of the passwords by users via commandline (should be discouraged); Other currently unknown false positives

**References:**
- https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/espionage-asia-governments
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/09/26/bumblebee-round-two/
- https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/10/25/dev-0832-vice-society-opportunistic-ransomware-campaigns-impacting-us-education-sector/
- https://www.huntress.com/blog/slashandgrab-screen-connect-post-exploitation-in-the-wild-cve-2024-1709-cve-2024-1708

---

## Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9fc51a3c-81b3-4fa7-b35f-7c02cf10fd2d` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059.001 |
| **Author** | James Pemberton / @4A616D6573, Endgame, JHasenbusch, oscd.community, Austin Songer @austinsonger |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_web_request_cmd_and_cmdlets.yml)**

> Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via CommandLine

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets
-- Sigma ID:     9fc51a3c-81b3-4fa7-b35f-7c02cf10fd2d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001
-- Author:       James Pemberton / @4A616D6573, Endgame, JHasenbusch, oscd.community, Austin Songer @austinsonger
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_web_request_cmd_and_cmdlets.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Use of Get-Command and Get-Help modules to reference Invoke-WebRequest and Start-BitsTransfer.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%[System.Net.WebRequest]::create%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%curl %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Invoke-RestMethod%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Invoke-WebRequest%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% irm %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%iwr %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Resume-BitsTransfer%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Start-BitsTransfer%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wget %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WinHttp.WinHttpRequest%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Use of Get-Command and Get-Help modules to reference Invoke-WebRequest and Start-BitsTransfer.

**References:**
- https://4sysops.com/archives/use-powershell-to-download-a-file-with-http-https-and-ftp/
- https://blog.jourdant.me/post/3-ways-to-download-files-with-powershell
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/bitstransfer/add-bitsfile?view=windowsserver2019-ps

| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1033 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_whoami_as_param.yml)**

> Detects a suspicious process command line that uses whoami as first parameter (as e.g. used by EfsPotato)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        WhoAmI as Parameter
-- Sigma ID:     e9142d84-fbe0-401d-ac50-3e519fb00c89
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1033
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe whoami%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

## Execution via WorkFolders.exe

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `0bbc6369-43e3-453d-9944-cae58821c173` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Maxime Thiebaut (@0xThiebaut) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_workfolders.yml)**

> Detects using WorkFolders.exe to execute an arbitrary control.exe

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Execution via WorkFolders.exe
-- Sigma ID:     0bbc6369-43e3-453d-9944-cae58821c173
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Maxime Thiebaut (@0xThiebaut)
-- Date:         2021-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_susp_workfolders.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of the uncommon Windows Work Folders feature.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\control.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WorkFolders.exe'))
  AND NOT (procName = 'C:\Windows\System32\control.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate usage of the uncommon Windows Work Folders feature.

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/elliotkillick/status/1449812843772227588

---

## Suspect Svchost Activity

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `16c37b52-b141-42a5-a3ea-bbe098444397` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1055 |
| **Author** | David Burkett, @signalblur |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_svchost_execution_with_no_cli_flags.yml)**

> It is extremely abnormal for svchost.exe to spawn without any CLI arguments and is normally observed when a malicious process spawns the process and injects code into the process memory space.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspect Svchost Activity
-- Sigma ID:     16c37b52-b141-42a5-a3ea-bbe098444397
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1055
-- Author:       David Burkett, @signalblur
-- Date:         2019-12-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_svchost_execution_with_no_cli_flags.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Rpcnet.exe / rpcnetp.exe which is a lojack style software. https://www.blackhat.com/docs/us-14/materials/us-14-Kamlyuk-Kamluk-Computrace-Backdoor-Revisited.pdf
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%svchost.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\svchost.exe')
  AND NOT (((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rpcnet.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rpcnetp.exe')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] = 'None'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Rpcnet.exe / rpcnetp.exe which is a lojack style software. https://www.blackhat.com/docs/us-14/materials/us-14-Kamlyuk-Kamluk-Computrace-Backdoor-Revisited.pdf

**References:**
- https://web.archive.org/web/20180718061628/https://securitybytes.io/blue-team-fundamentals-part-two-windows-processes-759fe15965e2

## Suspicious Process Masquerading As SvcHost.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `be58d2e2-06c8-4f58-b666-b99f6dc3b6cd` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036.005 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_svchost_masqueraded_execution.yml)**

> Detects a suspicious process that is masquerading as the legitimate "svchost.exe" by naming its binary "svchost.exe" and executing from an uncommon location.
Adversaries often disguise their malicious binaries by naming them after legitimate system processes like "svchost.exe" to evade detection.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Process Masquerading As SvcHost.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     be58d2e2-06c8-4f58-b666-b99f6dc3b6cd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036.005
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2024-08-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_svchost_masqueraded_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\svchost.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

**References:**
- https://tria.ge/240731-jh4crsycnb/behavioral2
- https://redcanary.com/blog/threat-detection/process-masquerading/

---

## Terminal Service Process Spawn

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `1012f107-b8f1-4271-af30-5aed2de89b39` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1190, T1210 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_svchost_termserv_proc_spawn.yml)**

> Detects a process spawned by the terminal service server process (this could be an indicator for an exploitation of CVE-2019-0708)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Terminal Service Process Spawn
-- Sigma ID:     1012f107-b8f1-4271-af30-5aed2de89b39
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1190, T1210
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_svchost_termserv_proc_spawn.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%\\svchost.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%termsvcs%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://securingtomorrow.mcafee.com/other-blogs/mcafee-labs/rdp-stands-for-really-do-patch-understanding-the-wormable-rdp-vulnerability-cve-2019-0708/

## Uncommon Svchost Command Line Parameter

> Detects instances of svchost.exe running with an unusual or uncommon command line parameter by excluding known legitimate or common patterns.
This could point at a file masquerading as svchost, a process injection, or hollowing of a legitimate svchost instance.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon Svchost Command Line Parameter
-- Sigma ID:     f17211f1-1f24-4d0c-829f-31e28dc93cdd

  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\svchost.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely

## Permission Check Via Accesschk.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c625d754-6a3d-4f65-9c9a-536aea960d37` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1069.001 |
| **Author** | Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_accesschk_check_permissions.yml)**

> Detects the usage of the "Accesschk" utility, an access and privilege audit tool developed by SysInternal and often being abused by attacker to verify process privileges

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Permission Check Via Accesschk.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     c625d754-6a3d-4f65-9c9a-536aea960d37
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1069.001
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-10-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_accesschk_check_permissions.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    System administrator Usage
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%uwcqv %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%kwsu %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%qwsu %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%uwdqs %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] LIKE '%AccessChk'))
  OR (rawEventMsg LIKE '%Reports effective permissions%')
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\accesschk.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\accesschk64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'accesschk.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** System administrator Usage

**References:**
- https://speakerdeck.com/heirhabarov/hunting-for-privilege-escalation-in-windows-environment?slide=43
- https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JGs-aKf2OtU&ab_channel=OFFZONEMOSCOW
- https://github.com/carlospolop/PEASS-ng/blob/fa0f2e17fbc1d86f1fd66338a40e665e7182501d/winPEAS/winPEASbat/winPEAS.bat
- https://github.com/gladiatx0r/Powerless/blob/04f553bbc0c65baf4e57344deff84e3f016e6b51/Powerless.bat

## Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9212f354-7775-4e28-9c9f-8f0a4544e664` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_adexplorer_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
-- Sigma ID:     9212f354-7775-4e28-9c9f-8f0a4544e664
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_adexplorer_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%snapshot%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\ADExp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64a.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'AdExp'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'Active Directory Editor')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'Sysinternals ADExplorer')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/5743766-Global-Threat-Report-2019.html
- https://learn.microsoft.com/de-de/sysinternals/downloads/adexplorer
- https://github.com/c3c/ADExplorerSnapshot.py/tree/f700904defac330802bbfedd1d8ffd9248f4ee24
- https://www.packetlabs.net/posts/scattered-spider-is-a-young-ransomware-gang-exploiting-large-corporations/
- https://www.nccgroup.com/us/research-blog/lapsus-recent-techniques-tactics-and-procedures/
- https://trustedsec.com/blog/adexplorer-on-engagements

---

## Suspicious Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `ef61af62-bc74-4f58-b49b-626448227652` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_adexplorer_susp_execution.yml)**

> Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database to a suspicious directory. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
-- Sigma ID:     ef61af62-bc74-4f58-b49b-626448227652
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_adexplorer_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%snapshot%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\ADExp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64a.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'AdExp'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'Active Directory Editor')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'Sysinternals ADExplorer'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Downloads\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Temp\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/5743766-Global-Threat-Report-2019.html
- https://learn.microsoft.com/de-de/sysinternals/downloads/adexplorer
- https://github.com/c3c/ADExplorerSnapshot.py/tree/f700904defac330802bbfedd1d8ffd9248f4ee24
- https://www.packetlabs.net/posts/scattered-spider-is-a-young-ransomware-gang-exploiting-large-corporations/
- https://www.nccgroup.com/us/research-blog/lapsus-recent-techniques-tactics-and-procedures/
- https://trustedsec.com/blog/adexplorer-on-engagements

## Potential Execution of Sysinternals Tools

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `7cccd811-7ae9-4ebe-9afd-cb5c406b824b` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1588.002 |
| **Author** | Markus Neis |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_eula_accepted.yml)**

> Detects command lines that contain the 'accepteula' flag which could be a sign of execution of one of the Sysinternals tools

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Execution of Sysinternals Tools
-- Sigma ID:     7cccd811-7ae9-4ebe-9afd-cb5c406b824b
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1588.002
-- Author:       Markus Neis
-- Date:         2017-08-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_eula_accepted.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of SysInternals tools; Programs that use the same command line flag
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -accepteula%'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of SysInternals tools; Programs that use the same command line flag

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/Moti_B/status/1008587936735035392

## Kernel Memory Dump Via LiveKD

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `c7746f1c-47d3-43d6-8c45-cd1e54b6b0a2` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_livekd_kernel_memory_dump.yml)**

> Detects execution of LiveKD with the "-m" flag to potentially dump the kernel memory

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Kernel Memory Dump Via LiveKD
-- Sigma ID:     c7746f1c-47d3-43d6-8c45-cd1e54b6b0a2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_livekd_kernel_memory_dump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely in production environment
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -m%'
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\livekd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\livekd64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'livekd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely in production environment

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/livekd
- https://4sysops.com/archives/creating-a-complete-memory-dump-without-a-blue-screen/
- https://kb.acronis.com/content/60892

## Procdump Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `2e65275c-8288-4ab4-aeb7-6274f58b6b20` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036, T1003.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump.yml)**

> Detects usage of the SysInternals Procdump utility

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Procdump Execution
-- Sigma ID:     2e65275c-8288-4ab4-aeb7-6274f58b6b20
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of procdump by a developer or administrator
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\procdump.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\procdump64.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of procdump by a developer or administrator

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/procdump

## Potential SysInternals ProcDump Evasion

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `79b06761-465f-4f88-9ef2-150e24d3d737` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036, T1003.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump_evasion.yml)**

> Detects uses of the SysInternals ProcDump utility in which ProcDump or its output get renamed, or a dump file is moved or copied to a different name

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential SysInternals ProcDump Evasion
-- Sigma ID:     79b06761-465f-4f88-9ef2-150e24d3d737
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-01-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump_evasion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives are expected in cases in which ProcDump just gets copied to a different directory without any renaming
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy procdump%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%move procdump%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dmp %')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%2.dmp%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lsass%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%out.dmp%')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy lsass.exe\_%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%move lsass.exe\_%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False positives are expected in cases in which ProcDump just gets copied to a different directory without any renaming

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/mrd0x/status/1480785527901204481

---

## Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `5afee48e-67dd-4e03-a783-f74259dcf998` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036, T1003.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | stable |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump_lsass.yml)**

> Detects potential credential harvesting attempts through LSASS memory dumps using ProcDump.
This rule identifies suspicious command-line patterns that combine memory dump flags (-ma, -mm, -mp) with LSASS-related process markers.
LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) contains sensitive authentication data including plaintext passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets in memory.
Attackers commonly dump LSASS memory to extract credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump
-- Sigma ID:     5afee48e-67dd-4e03-a783-f74259dcf998
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump_lsass.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely, because no one should dump an lsass process memory; Another tool that uses command line flags similar to ProcDump
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((rawEventMsg LIKE '% -ma %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -mm %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -mp %')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ls%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% keyiso%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% samss%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unlikely, because no one should dump an lsass process memory; Another tool that uses command line flags similar to ProcDump

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/procdump
- https://research.splunk.com/endpoint/3742ebfe-64c2-11eb-ae93-0242ac130002
- https://x.com/wietze/status/1958302556033065292?s=12

---

> Detects user accept agreement execution in psexec commandline

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Psexec Execution
-- Sigma ID:     730fc21b-eaff-474b-ad23-90fd265d4988
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1569, T1021

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
```

## PsExec/PAExec Escalation to LOCAL SYSTEM

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `8834e2f7-6b4b-4f09-8906-d2276470ee23` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1587.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_paexec_escalate_system.yml)**

> Detects suspicious commandline flags used by PsExec and PAExec to escalate a command line to LOCAL_SYSTEM rights

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PsExec/PAExec Escalation to LOCAL SYSTEM
-- Sigma ID:     8834e2f7-6b4b-4f09-8906-d2276470ee23
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1587.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-11-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_paexec_escalate_system.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Admins that use PsExec or PAExec to escalate to the SYSTEM account for maintenance purposes (rare); Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%psexec%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%paexec%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%accepteula%'))
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s powershell%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i powershell%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s powershell%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Admins that use PsExec or PAExec to escalate to the SYSTEM account for maintenance purposes (rare); Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/psexec
- https://www.poweradmin.com/paexec/
- https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2020/10/kegtap-and-singlemalt-with-a-ransomware-chaser.html

## Potential PsExec Remote Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `ea011323-7045-460b-b2d7-0f7442ea6b38` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1587.001 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_remote_execution.yml)**

> Detects potential psexec command that initiate execution on a remote systems via common commandline flags used by the utility

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential PsExec Remote Execution
-- Sigma ID:     ea011323-7045-460b-b2d7-0f7442ea6b38
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1587.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_remote_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%accepteula%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -u %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -p %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/psexec
- https://www.poweradmin.com/paexec/
- https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2020/10/kegtap-and-singlemalt-with-a-ransomware-chaser.html

## PsExec Service Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `fdfcbd78-48f1-4a4b-90ac-d82241e368c5` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Thomas Patzke, Romaissa Adjailia, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexesvc.yml)**

> Detects launch of the PSEXESVC service, which means that this system was the target of a psexec remote execution

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PsExec Service Execution
-- Sigma ID:     fdfcbd78-48f1-4a4b-90ac-d82241e368c5
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Thomas Patzke, Romaissa Adjailia, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2017-06-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexesvc.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrative tasks
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName = 'C:\Windows\PSEXESVC.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'psexesvc.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate administrative tasks

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/psexec
- https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ro2QuZTIMBM
