## Suspicious UltraVNC Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `871b9555-69ca-4993-99d3-35a59f9f3599` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1021.005 |
| **Author** | Bhabesh Raj |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ultravnc_susp_execution.yml)**

> Detects suspicious UltraVNC command line flag combination that indicate a auto reconnect upon execution, e.g. startup (as seen being used by Gamaredon threat group)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious UltraVNC Execution
-- Sigma ID:     871b9555-69ca-4993-99d3-35a59f9f3599
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.005
-- Author:       Bhabesh Raj
-- Date:         2022-03-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ultravnc_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-autoreconnect %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-connect %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-id:%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://web.archive.org/web/20220224045756/https://www.ria.ee/sites/default/files/content-editors/kuberturve/tale_of_gamaredon_infection.pdf
- https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/shuckworm-gamaredon-espionage-ukraine
- https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/unit-42-title-gamaredon-group-toolset-evolution
- https://uvnc.com/docs/uvnc-viewer/52-ultravnc-viewer-commandline-parameters.html

## Uninstall Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `f0f7be61-9cf5-43be-9836-99d6ef448a18` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uninstall_crowdstrike_falcon.yml)**

> Adversaries may disable security tools to avoid possible detection of their tools and activities by uninstalling Crowdstrike Falcon

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uninstall Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor
-- Sigma ID:     f0f7be61-9cf5-43be-9836-99d6ef448a18
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-07-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uninstall_crowdstrike_falcon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrator might leverage the same command line for debugging or other purposes. However this action must be always investigated
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WindowsSensor.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /uninstall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /quiet%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Administrator might leverage the same command line for debugging or other purposes. However this action must be always investigated

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1562.001/T1562.001.md

## User Shell Folders Registry Modification via CommandLine

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `8f3ab69a-aa22-4943-aa58-e0a52fdf6818` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1547.001, T1112 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_user_shell_folders_registry_modification.yml)**

> Detects modifications to User Shell Folders registry values via reg.exe or PowerShell, which could indicate persistence attempts.
Attackers may modify User Shell Folders registry values to point to malicious executables or scripts that will be executed during startup.
This technique is often used to maintain persistence on a compromised system by ensuring that malicious payloads are executed automatically.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        User Shell Folders Registry Modification via CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     8f3ab69a-aa22-4943-aa58-e0a52fdf6818
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1547.001, T1112
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-01-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_user_shell_folders_registry_modification.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Usage of reg.exe or PowerShell to modify User Shell Folders for legitimate purposes; but rare.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-ItemProperty%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Set-ItemProperty%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%si %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Shell Folders%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\User Shell Folders%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Startup%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('powershell.exe', 'pwsh.dll', 'reg.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Usage of reg.exe or PowerShell to modify User Shell Folders for legitimate purposes; but rare.

**References:**
- https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/muddywater-snakes-riverbank/

---

## Uncommon Userinit Child Process

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `0a98a10c-685d-4ab0-bddc-b6bdd1d48458` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1037.001 |
| **Author** | Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure), Tim Shelton |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_userinit_uncommon_child_processes.yml)**

> Detects uncommon "userinit.exe" child processes, which could be a sign of uncommon shells or login scripts used for persistence.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon Userinit Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     0a98a10c-685d-4ab0-bddc-b6bdd1d48458
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1037.001
-- Author:       Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure), Tim Shelton
-- Date:         2019-01-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_userinit_uncommon_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate logon scripts or custom shells may trigger false positives. Apply additional filters accordingly.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\userinit.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate logon scripts or custom shells may trigger false positives. Apply additional filters accordingly.

**References:**
- https://cocomelonc.github.io/persistence/2022/12/09/malware-pers-20.html
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/server-core/server-core-sconfig#powershell-is-the-default-shell-on-server-core

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `58f50261-c53b-4c88-bd12-1d71f12eda4c` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1555.004 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vaultcmd_list_creds.yml)**

> List credentials currently stored in Windows Credential Manager via the native Windows utility vaultcmd.exe

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
-- Sigma ID:     58f50261-c53b-4c88-bd12-1d71f12eda4c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1555.004
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-04-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vaultcmd_list_creds.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)

-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR

  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VaultCmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
```

## Registry Modification Attempt Via VBScript

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `921aa10f-2e74-4cca-9498-98f9ca4d6fdf` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence, execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1112, T1059.005 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vbscript_registry_modification.yml)**

> Detects attempts to modify the registry using VBScript's CreateObject("Wscript.shell") and RegWrite methods via common LOLBINs.
It could be an attempt to modify the registry for persistence without using straightforward methods like regedit.exe, reg.exe, or PowerShell.
Threat Actors may use this technique to evade detection by security solutions that monitor for direct registry modifications through traditional tools.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Registry Modification Attempt Via VBScript
-- Sigma ID:     921aa10f-2e74-4cca-9498-98f9ca4d6fdf
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence, execution | T1112, T1059.005
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-08-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vbscript_registry_modification.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%CreateObject%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Wscript.shell%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.RegWrite%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mauricefielenbach_livingofftheland-redteam-persistence-activity-7344801774182051843-TE00/
- https://www.nextron-systems.com/2025/07/29/detecting-the-most-popular-mitre-persistence-method-registry-run-keys-startup-folder/

## Verclsid.exe Runs COM Object

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `d06be4b9-8045-428b-a567-740a26d9db25` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Victor Sergeev, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_verclsid_runs_com.yml)**

> Detects when verclsid.exe is used to run COM object via GUID

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Verclsid.exe Runs COM Object
-- Sigma ID:     d06be4b9-8045-428b-a567-740a26d9db25
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_verclsid_runs_com.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Verclsid/
- https://gist.github.com/NickTyrer/0598b60112eaafe6d07789f7964290d5
- https://bohops.com/2018/08/18/abusing-the-com-registry-structure-part-2-loading-techniques-for-evasion-and-persistence/

## Virtualbox Driver Installation or Starting of VMs

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `bab049ca-7471-4828-9024-38279a4c04da` |
| **Level** | low |
| **FSM Severity** | 3 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1564.006, T1564 |
| **Author** | Janantha Marasinghe |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_virtualbox_execution.yml)**

> Adversaries can carry out malicious operations using a virtual instance to avoid detection. This rule is built to detect the registration of the Virtualbox driver or start of a Virtualbox VM.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Virtualbox Driver Installation or Starting of VMs
-- Sigma ID:     bab049ca-7471-4828-9024-38279a4c04da
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1564.006, T1564
-- Author:       Janantha Marasinghe
-- Date:         2020-09-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_virtualbox_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    This may have false positives on hosts where Virtualbox is legitimately being used for operations
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VBoxRT.dll,RTR3Init%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VBoxC.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VBoxDrv.sys%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%startvm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%controlvm%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** This may have false positives on hosts where Virtualbox is legitimately being used for operations

**References:**
- https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2020/05/21/ragnar-locker-ransomware-deploys-virtual-machine-to-dodge-security/
- https://threatpost.com/maze-ransomware-ragnar-locker-virtual-machine/159350/

---

## Suspicious VBoxDrvInst.exe Parameters

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `b7b19cb6-9b32-4fc4-a108-73f19acfe262` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1112 |
| **Author** | Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_virtualbox_vboxdrvinst_execution.yml)**

> Detect VBoxDrvInst.exe run with parameters allowing processing INF file.
This allows to create values in the registry and install drivers.
For example one could use this technique to obtain persistence via modifying one of Run or RunOnce registry keys


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious VBoxDrvInst.exe Parameters
-- Sigma ID:     b7b19cb6-9b32-4fc4-a108-73f19acfe262
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_virtualbox_vboxdrvinst_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of VBoxDrvInst.exe utility by VirtualBox Guest Additions installation process
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VBoxDrvInst.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%driver%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%executeinf%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of VBoxDrvInst.exe utility by VirtualBox Guest Additions installation process

**References:**
- https://github.com/LOLBAS-Project/LOLBAS/blob/4db780e0f0b2e2bb8cb1fa13e09196da9b9f1834/yml/LOLUtilz/OtherBinaries/VBoxDrvInst.yml
- https://twitter.com/pabraeken/status/993497996179492864

---

## Potential Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `7aa4e81a-a65c-4e10-9f81-b200eb229d7d` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution, persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_toolbox_cmd_persistence.yml)**

> Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script to run for a specific VM state

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
-- Sigma ID:     7aa4e81a-a65c-4e10-9f81-b200eb229d7d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution, persistence | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_toolbox_cmd_persistence.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% script %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% set %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'toolbox-cmd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://bohops.com/2021/10/08/analyzing-and-detecting-a-vmtools-persistence-technique/
- https://www.hexacorn.com/blog/2017/01/14/beyond-good-ol-run-key-part-53/

## Suspicious Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `236d8e89-ed95-4789-a982-36f4643738ba` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution, persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_toolbox_cmd_persistence_susp.yml)**

> Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script that's located in a potentially suspicious location to run for a specific VM state

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
-- Sigma ID:     236d8e89-ed95-4789-a982-36f4643738ba
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution, persistence | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_toolbox_cmd_persistence_susp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% script %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% set %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'toolbox-cmd.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\PerfLogs\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://bohops.com/2021/10/08/analyzing-and-detecting-a-vmtools-persistence-technique/

## Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of VsCode

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `5a3164f2-b373-4152-93cf-090b13c12d27` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218, T1202 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_child_processes_anomalies.yml)**

> Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes spawning from a VsCode "code.exe" process. This could indicate an attempt of persistence via VsCode tasks or terminal profiles.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of VsCode
-- Sigma ID:     5a3164f2-b373-4152-93cf-090b13c12d27
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218, T1202
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_child_processes_anomalies.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    In development environment where VsCode is used heavily. False positives may occur when developers use task to compile or execute different types of code. Remove or add processes accordingly
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\code.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** In development environment where VsCode is used heavily. False positives may occur when developers use task to compile or execute different types of code. Remove or add processes accordingly

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/nas_bench/status/1618021838407495681
- https://twitter.com/nas_bench/status/1618021415852335105

---

## Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `90d6bd71-dffb-4989-8d86-a827fedd6624` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1071.001, T1219 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), citron_ninja |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_execution.yml)**

> Detects Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
-- Sigma ID:     90d6bd71-dffb-4989-8d86-a827fedd6624
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1071.001, T1219
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), citron_ninja
-- Date:         2023-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'None')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe tunnel'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% tunnel')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/d /c %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\servers\\Stable-%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%code-server.cmd%'))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe tunnel%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--accept-server-license-terms%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel

**References:**
- https://ipfyx.fr/post/visual-studio-code-tunnel/
- https://badoption.eu/blog/2023/01/31/code_c2.html
- https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/remote/tunnels

---

## Visual Studio Code Tunnel Shell Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `f4a623c2-4ef5-4c33-b811-0642f702c9f1` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1071.001 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_remote_shell_.yml)**

> Detects the execution of a shell (powershell, bash, wsl...) via Visual Studio Code tunnel. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel and execute arbitrary commands on the system.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Visual Studio Code Tunnel Shell Execution
-- Sigma ID:     f4a623c2-4ef5-4c33-b811-0642f702c9f1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1071.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_remote_shell_.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel and running code from there
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\servers\\Stable-%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\server\\node.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%.vscode-server%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel and running code from there

**References:**
- https://ipfyx.fr/post/visual-studio-code-tunnel/
- https://badoption.eu/blog/2023/01/31/code_c2.html
- https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/remote/tunnels

## Visual Studio Code Tunnel Service Installation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `30bf1789-379d-4fdc-900f-55cd0a90a801` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1071.001 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_service_install.yml)**

> Detects the installation of VsCode tunnel (code-tunnel) as a service.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Visual Studio Code Tunnel Service Installation
-- Sigma ID:     30bf1789-379d-4fdc-900f-55cd0a90a801
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1071.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_service_install.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate installation of code-tunnel as a service
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tunnel %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%service%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%internal-run%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tunnel-service.log%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate installation of code-tunnel as a service

**References:**
- https://ipfyx.fr/post/visual-studio-code-tunnel/
- https://badoption.eu/blog/2023/01/31/code_c2.html
- https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/remote/tunnels

## Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via VSDiagnostics.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `ac1c92b4-ac81-405a-9978-4604d78cc47e` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vsdiagnostics_execution_proxy.yml)**

> Detects execution of "VSDiagnostics.exe" with the "start" command in order to launch and proxy arbitrary binaries.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via VSDiagnostics.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     ac1c92b4-ac81-405a-9978-4604d78cc47e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-08-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vsdiagnostics_execution_proxy.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage for tracing and diagnostics purposes
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /launch:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -launch:%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%start%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VSDiagnostics.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'VSDiagnostics.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate usage for tracing and diagnostics purposes

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/0xBoku/status/1679200664013135872

---

## Proxy Execution via Vshadow

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `d7c75059-2901-4578-b209-8837fd31c6a8` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1202 |
| **Author** | David Faiss |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vshadow_exec.yml)**

> Detects the invocation of vshadow.exe with the -exec parameter that executes a specified script or command after the shadow copies are created but before the VShadow tool exits.
VShadow is a command-line tool that you can use to create and manage volume shadow copies. While legitimate backup or administrative scripts may use this flag,
attackers can leverage this parameter to proxy the execution of malware.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Proxy Execution via Vshadow
-- Sigma ID:     d7c75059-2901-4578-b209-8837fd31c6a8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       David Faiss
-- Date:         2025-05-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vshadow_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    System backup or administrator tools; Legitimate administrative scripts
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-exec%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\vshadow.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'vshadow.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** System backup or administrator tools; Legitimate administrative scripts

**References:**
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/OtherMSBinaries/Vshadow/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/vss/vshadow-tool-and-sample

---

## Suspicious Vsls-Agent Command With AgentExtensionPath Load

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `43103702-5886-11ed-9b6a-0242ac120002` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1218 |
| **Author** | bohops |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vslsagent_agentextensionpath_load.yml)**

> Detects Microsoft Visual Studio vsls-agent.exe lolbin execution with a suspicious library load using the --agentExtensionPath parameter

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Vsls-Agent Command With AgentExtensionPath Load
-- Sigma ID:     43103702-5886-11ed-9b6a-0242ac120002
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       bohops
-- Date:         2022-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vslsagent_agentextensionpath_load.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on custom use of vsls-agent.exe
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\vsls-agent.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--agentExtensionPath%'))
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Microsoft.VisualStudio.LiveShare.Agent.%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** False positives depend on custom use of vsls-agent.exe

**References:**
- https://twitter.com/bohops/status/1583916360404729857

---

## Vulnerable Driver Blocklist Registry Tampering Via CommandLine

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `22154f0e-5132-4a54-aa78-cc62f6def531` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1562.001 |
| **Author** | Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | experimental |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vulnerable_driver_blocklist_registry_tampering.yml)**

> Detects tampering of the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist registry via command line tools such as PowerShell or REG.EXE.
The Vulnerable Driver Blocklist is a security feature that helps prevent the loading of known vulnerable drivers.
Disabling this feature may indicate an attempt to bypass security controls, often targeted by threat actors
to facilitate the installation of malicious or vulnerable drivers, particularly in scenarios involving Endpoint Detection and Response


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Vulnerable Driver Blocklist Registry Tampering Via CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     22154f0e-5132-4a54-aa78-cc62f6def531
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-01-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vulnerable_driver_blocklist_registry_tampering.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    It is very unlikely for legitimate activities to disable the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist via command line tools; thus it is recommended to investigate promptly.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-ItemProperty %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Set-ItemProperty %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%si %'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Control\\CI\\Config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VulnerableDriverBlocklistEnable%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll', 'reg.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** It is very unlikely for legitimate activities to disable the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist via command line tools; thus it is recommended to investigate promptly.

**References:**
- https://www.sophos.com/en-us/blog/sharpening-the-knife-gold-blades-strategic-evolution
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/application-security/application-control/app-control-for-business/design/microsoft-recommended-driver-block-rules

## Use of W32tm as Timer

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `6da2c9f5-7c53-401b-aacb-92c040ce1215` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1124 |
| **Author** | frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_w32tm.yml)**

> When configured with suitable command line arguments, w32tm can act as a delay mechanism

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Use of W32tm as Timer
-- Sigma ID:     6da2c9f5-7c53-401b-aacb-92c040ce1215
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1124
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-09-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_w32tm.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/stripchart%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/computer:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/period:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/dataonly%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/samples:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\w32tm.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'w32time.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate use

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/d0dad62dbcae9c60c519368e82c196a3db577055/atomics/T1124/T1124.md

## Wab Execution From Non Default Location

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `395907ee-96e5-4666-af2e-2ca91688e151` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wab_execution_from_non_default_location.yml)**

> Detects execution of wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) from non default locations as seen with bumblebee activity

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Wab Execution From Non Default Location
-- Sigma ID:     395907ee-96e5-4666-af2e-2ca91688e151
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wab_execution_from_non_default_location.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\wab.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wabmig.exe')
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\Windows Mail\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Mail\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/08/08/bumblebee-roasts-its-way-to-domain-admin/
- https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/bumblebee-loader-cybercrime
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/09/26/bumblebee-round-two/

## Wab/Wabmig Unusual Parent Or Child Processes

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `63d1ccc0-2a43-4f4b-9289-361b308991ff` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wab_unusual_parents.yml)**

> Detects unusual parent or children of the wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) processes as seen being used with bumblebee activity

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Wab/Wabmig Unusual Parent Or Child Processes
-- Sigma ID:     63d1ccc0-2a43-4f4b-9289-361b308991ff
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wab_unusual_parents.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wab.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wabmig.exe'))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WmiPrvSE.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\svchost.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe'))
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\wab.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wabmig.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/08/08/bumblebee-roasts-its-way-to-domain-admin/
- https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/bumblebee-loader-cybercrime
- https://thedfirreport.com/2022/09/26/bumblebee-round-two/

## All Backups Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `639c9081-f482-47d3-a0bd-ddee3d4ecd76` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1490 |
| **Author** | frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_delete_all_backups.yml)**

> Detects the deletion of all backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe".
This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors.
This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        All Backups Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     639c9081-f482-47d3-a0bd-ddee3d4ecd76
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_delete_all_backups.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%delete%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%backup%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%keepVersions:0%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1490/T1490.md#atomic-test-5---windows---delete-volume-shadow-copies-via-wmi-with-powershell
- https://github.com/albertzsigovits/malware-notes/blob/558898932c1579ff589290092a2c8febefc3a4c9/Ransomware/Lockbit.md
- https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/ranzy-ransomware-better-encryption-among-new-features-of-thunderx-derivative/
- https://www.trendmicro.com/vinfo/us/security/news/cybercrime-and-digital-threats/ransomware-report-avaddon-and-new-techniques-emerge-industrial-sector-targeted
- https://www.trendmicro.com/content/dam/trendmicro/global/en/research/24/b/lockbit-attempts-to-stay-afloat-with-a-new-version/technical-appendix-lockbit-ng-dev-analysis.pdf
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/wbadmin-delete-systemstatebackup

> Detects the deletion of backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe".
This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors.
This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Backup Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     89f75308-5b1b-4390-b2d8-d6b2340efaf8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_delete_backups.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate backup activity from administration scripts and software.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate backup activity from administration scripts and software.

**References:**
- https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/blob/f339e7da7d05f6057fdfcdd3742bfcf365fee2a9/atomics/T1490/T1490.md#atomic-test-5---windows---delete-volume-shadow-copies-via-wmi-with-powershell
- https://github.com/albertzsigovits/malware-notes/blob/558898932c1579ff589290092a2c8febefc3a4c9/Ransomware/Lockbit.md
- https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/ranzy-ransomware-better-encryption-among-new-features-of-thunderx-derivative/
- https://www.trendmicro.com/vinfo/us/security/news/cybercrime-and-digital-threats/ransomware-report-avaddon-and-new-techniques-emerge-industrial-sector-targeted
- https://www.trendmicro.com/content/dam/trendmicro/global/en/research/24/b/lockbit-attempts-to-stay-afloat-with-a-new-version/technical-appendix-lockbit-ng-dev-analysis.pdf
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/wbadmin-delete-systemstatebackup

## Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `8b93a509-1cb8-42e1-97aa-ee24224cdc15` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003.003 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_dump_sensitive_files.yml)**

> Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     8b93a509-1cb8-42e1-97aa-ee24224cdc15
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2024-05-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_dump_sensitive_files.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate backup operation by authorized administrators. Matches must be investigated and allowed on a case by case basis.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%start%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%backup%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SAM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SECURITY%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SYSTEM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\NTDS\\NTDS.dit%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Legitimate backup operation by authorized administrators. Matches must be investigated and allowed on a case by case basis.

**References:**
- https://github.com/LOLBAS-Project/LOLBAS/blob/2cc01b01132b5c304027a658c698ae09dd6a92bf/yml/OSBinaries/Wbadmin.yml
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Wbadmin/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/wbadmin-start-recovery
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/wbadmin-start-backup

---

## File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `6fe4aa1e-0531-4510-8be2-782154b73b48` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | impact |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1490 |
| **Author** | Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_restore_file.yml)**

> Detects the recovery of files from backups via "wbadmin.exe".
Attackers can restore sensitive files such as NTDS.DIT or Registry Hives from backups in order to potentially extract credentials.


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     6fe4aa1e-0531-4510-8be2-782154b73b48
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2024-05-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_restore_file.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% recovery%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%recoveryTarget%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%itemtype:File%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/wbadmin-start-recovery
- https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Binaries/Wbadmin/

---

```sql
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
```

## Potentially Suspicious WebDAV LNK Execution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `1412aa78-a24c-4abd-83df-767dfb2c5bbe` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1059.001, T1204 |
| **Author** | Micah Babinski |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webdav_lnk_execution.yml)**

> Detects possible execution via LNK file accessed on a WebDAV server.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious WebDAV LNK Execution
-- Sigma ID:     1412aa78-a24c-4abd-83df-767dfb2c5bbe
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001, T1204
-- Author:       Micah Babinski
-- Date:         2023-08-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webdav_lnk_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\explorer.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\DavWWWRoot\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.trellix.com/en-us/about/newsroom/stories/research/beyond-file-search-a-novel-method.html
- https://micahbabinski.medium.com/search-ms-webdav-and-chill-99c5b23ac462

## Chopper Webshell Process Pattern

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `fa3c117a-bc0d-416e-a31b-0c0e80653efb` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence, discovery |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087 |
| **Author** | Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), MSTI (query) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_chopper.yml)**

> Detects patterns found in process executions cause by China Chopper like tiny (ASPX) webshells

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Chopper Webshell Process Pattern
-- Sigma ID:     fa3c117a-bc0d-416e-a31b-0c0e80653efb
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, discovery | T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), MSTI (query)
-- Date:         2022-10-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_chopper.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&ipconfig&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&quser&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&whoami&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&c:&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&cd&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&dir&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&echo [E]%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&echo [S]%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\w3wp.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\w3wp.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/09/30/analyzing-attacks-using-the-exchange-vulnerabilities-cve-2022-41040-and-cve-2022-41082/

## Suspicious Process By Web Server Process

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `8202070f-edeb-4d31-a010-a26c72ac5600` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | persistence |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1505.003, T1190 |
| **Author** | Thomas Patzke, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Zach Stanford @svch0st, Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_susp_process_spawned_from_webserver.yml)**

> Detects potentially suspicious processes being spawned by a web server process which could be the result of a successfully placed web shell or exploitation


```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Process By Web Server Process
-- Sigma ID:     8202070f-edeb-4d31-a010-a26c72ac5600
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.003, T1190
-- Author:       Thomas Patzke, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Zach Stanford @svch0st, Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_susp_process_spawned_from_webserver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Particular web applications may spawn a shell process legitimately
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\arp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\at.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dsget.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\hostname.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nbtstat.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netdom.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ntdsutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\qprocess.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\query.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\qwinsta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wusa.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Particular web applications may spawn a shell process legitimately

**References:**
- https://media.defense.gov/2020/Jun/09/2002313081/-1/-1/0/CSI-DETECT-AND-PREVENT-WEB-SHELL-MALWARE-20200422.PDF

---

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Webshell Tool Reconnaissance Activity
-- Sigma ID:     f64e5c19-879c-4bae-b471-6d84c8339677
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.003

-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%perl --help%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%perl -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python --help%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python3 --help%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python3 -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wget --help%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://ragged-lab.blogspot.com/2020/07/webshells-automating-reconnaissance.html

## Potential Credential Dumping Via WER

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `9a4ccd1a-3526-4d99-b980-9f9c5d3a6ff3` |
| **Level** | high |
| **FSM Severity** | 7 |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1003.001 |
| **Author** | @pbssubhash , Nasreddine Bencherchali |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfault_lsass_shtinkering.yml)**

> Detects potential credential dumping via Windows Error Reporting LSASS Shtinkering technique which uses the Windows Error Reporting to dump lsass

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Credential Dumping Via WER
-- Sigma ID:     9a4ccd1a-3526-4d99-b980-9f9c5d3a6ff3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.001
-- Author:       @pbssubhash , Nasreddine Bencherchali
-- Date:         2022-12-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfault_lsass_shtinkering.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Windows Error Reporting might produce similar behavior. In that case, check the PID associated with the "-p" parameter in the CommandLine.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Windows Error Reporting might produce similar behavior. In that case, check the PID associated with the "-p" parameter in the CommandLine.

**References:**
- https://github.com/deepinstinct/Lsass-Shtinkering
- https://media.defcon.org/DEF%20CON%2030/DEF%20CON%2030%20presentations/Asaf%20Gilboa%20-%20LSASS%20Shtinkering%20Abusing%20Windows%20Error%20Reporting%20to%20Dump%20LSASS.pdf

## Potential ReflectDebugger Content Execution Via WerFault.EXE

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Sigma ID** | `fabfb3a7-3ce1-4445-9c7c-3c27f1051cdd` |
| **Level** | medium |
| **FSM Severity** | 5 |
| **MITRE Tactics** | execution |
| **MITRE Techniques** | T1036 |
| **Author** | X__Junior (Nextron Systems) |
| **Status** | test |

**[View on GitHub ↗](https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfault_reflect_debugger_exec.yml)**

> Detects execution of "WerFault.exe" with the "-pr" commandline flag that is used to run files stored in the ReflectDebugger key which could be used to store the path to the malware in order to masquerade the execution flow

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential ReflectDebugger Content Execution Via WerFault.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     fabfb3a7-3ce1-4445-9c7c-3c27f1051cdd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1036
-- Author:       X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfault_reflect_debugger_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -pr %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\WerFault.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WerFault.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
```

**False Positives:** Unknown

**References:**
- https://cocomelonc.github.io/malware/2022/11/02/malware-pers-18.html
- https://www.hexacorn.com/blog/2018/08/31/beyond-good-ol-run-key-part-85/
